The report sends a stark message on the threat of noncommunicable diseases, which take an immense and increasing toll on lives, livelihoods, health systems, communities, economies and societies,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “The report calls for a substantial increase in investments in health and health systems to get back on track towards the Sustainable Development Goals.” NCDs ̶ an ever-increasing health threat for future generationsDespite overall health progress, the share of deaths caused annually by NCDs has grown consistently and is now claiming nearly three quarters of all lives lost each year. If this trend continues, NCDs are projected to account for about 86% of the 90 million annual deaths by mid-century; consequently, 77 million of these will be due to NCDs – a nearly 90% increase in absolute numbers since 2019. Stagnating progress calls for acceleration More recent trends show signs of slowdown in the annual rate of reduction (ARR) for many indicators. For example, the global maternal mortality ratio needs to decline by 11.6% per year between 2021 and 2030 to meet the SDG target. Similarly, the net reduction in TB incidence from 2015 to 2021 was only one-fifth of the way to the 2025 milestone of WHO’s End TB Strategy.
translations.hypothesis: Furthermore, the global health crisis has highlighted the importance of health equity and has contributed to the widening gap in access to quality healthcare, essential medicines, and vaccines. As a result, there have been significant changes in the prevalence of infectious diseases and non-communicable diseases (NCDs), and some populations are particularly vulnerable to the effects of these diseases. The World Health Organization (WHO) conducts a global health survey every year to assess the state of health worldwide. In a recent report, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO, warned that the global health crisis poses a major threat to human health, livelihoods, and the global economy. The report called for increased investment in health systems to ensure a sustainable recovery. NCDs are a growing public health challenge, and the burden of NCDs is increasing in many countries. The World Health Organization estimates that NCDs are responsible for more than 70% of all deaths worldwide. If this trend continues, NCDs are expected to account for more than 80% of all deaths by 2030.

translations.source: Duk da raguwar kamuwa da haɗarin kiwon lafiya da yawa kamar shan taba, shan barasa, tashin hankali, rashin tsaftataccen ruwa da tsaftar muhalli, da tsaftar yara ci gaban bai wadatar ba kuma fuskantar wasu haɗari kamar gurɓataccen iska ya kasance mai girma. Abin mamaki shi ne, yawan ƙiba yana ƙaruwa ba tare da wata alama ta koma baya ba. Bugu da ƙari kuma, faɗaɗa damar samun muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya ya ragu idan aka kwatanta da ribar da aka samu kafin shekarar 2015, ba tare da wani gagarumin ci gaba ba wajen rage wahalhalun kuɗi saboda farashin kula da lafiya ba. Wannan yana matuƙar iyakance ikonmu don cimma Taimakon Kiwon Lafiya na Duniya nan da 2030. Annobar Covid-19 muhimmiyar tunatarwa ce ga Cigaban da aka samu ba na layi ba ne kuma ba tabbas ba ne, in ji Dr Samira Asma, Mataimakiyar Darakta-Janar ta WHO kan Bayanai, Bincike da Isar da Taimako don samun tasiri. Don tsayawa kan hanya zuwa ajandar SDG ta 2030, dole ne mu yi aiki da gaske kuma tare don isar da tasiri a duk ƙasashe. Rahoton wannan shekara a karon farko ya kunshi wani sashe na musamman kan sauyin yanayi da lafiya, kuma muna sa ran cewa wannan zai fi dacewa a cikin rahoton da ke gaba. Don wannan batu da duk sauran fannonin lokaci, amintattun bayanai da aka rarraba suna da mahimmanci don bin diddigin ci gaba da inganta manufofin kiwon lafiya na ƙasa da na duniya. Bayanan Edita: Rahoton Kididdigar Kiwon Lafiyar Duniya shi ne Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta tattara bayanai na baya-bayan nan kan abubuwan da suka shafi lafiya da makamantansu. Don tambayoyi, tuntuɓi [email protected]
translations.reference: Despite a reduction in exposure to many health risks – such as tobacco use, alcohol consumption, violence, unsafe water and sanitation, and child stunting – progress was inadequate and exposure to some risks such as air pollution remains high. Alarmingly, the prevalence of obesity is rising with no immediate sign of reversal. Furthermore, expanded access to essential health services has slowed compared to pre-2015 gains, coupled with no significant progress in reducing financial hardship due to health-care costs. This drastically limits our ability to achieve Universal Health Coverage by 2030. “The COVID-19 pandemic is an important reminder that progress is neither linear nor guaranteed,” warns Dr Samira Asma, WHO Assistant Director-General for Data, Analytics and Delivery for Impact. “To stay on track towards the 2030 SDG agenda, we must act decisively and collectively to deliver a measurable impact in all countries.” This year’s report includes for the first time a dedicated section on climate change and health, and we anticipate that this will be of more relevance in the report going forward. For this issue and all other areas timely, reliable and disaggregated data are critical to track progress and improve national and global health policies. Editor’s note: The World Health Statistics report is the World Health Organization’s (WHO) annual compilation of the most recent available data on health and health-related indicators. For inquiries, contact [email protected]
translations.hypothesis: Despite the significant progress made in healthcare over the years, including advancements in medical technology, improved sanitation, and increased access to healthcare services, many challenges remain. These include the high cost of healthcare, limited access to essential medicines, and the growing burden of non-communicable diseases such as heart disease, stroke, and cancer. Furthermore, the COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the fragility of our healthcare systems and the need for greater investment in public health infrastructure. According to Dr. Samira Asma, Deputy Director-General of the WHO, "The COVID-19 pandemic has been a stark reminder that progress is not always linear and that we must be prepared for future health crises." To achieve the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs), particularly SDG 3, which aims to ensure healthy lives and promote well-being for all, we must work together to address these challenges and ensure that everyone has access to quality healthcare. This year's report includes a special section on the links between climate change and health, and we hope that this will be included in future reports. As with all other areas of development, reliable data is essential for tracking progress and informing policy decisions.

translations.source: Darakta-Janar na WHO ya sanar da lambar yabo ta Shugabannin Lafiya ta Duniya Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum da Peter Piot sun samu lambar yabo don sadaukar da kai ga harkokin lafiya Darakta-Janar na WHO Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus zai ba da lambobin yabon na don yaba wa gudummawar da aka bayar don ciyar da lafiyar duniya gaba na tsawon rayuwa a lokacin babban taron buɗe taron Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya karo na saba'in da shida. Za a ba da lambar Yabon ta Darakta-Janar ta Shugabannin Duniya, wacce aka kafa a cikin 2019 ga Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum da Peter Piot. Mutanen biyu da za a ba wa lambar yabon dai fitattun masana kimiyya ne da kuma shugabannin kiwon lafiyar jama'a waɗanda ke da hannu wajen gano cutar Ebola kafin su kai ga matsayin jagoranci a fannin kiwon lafiyar duniya. Lambar yabon wata alamar gimamawa ce ga nasarorin da suka samu a rayuwarsu a fannin lafiyar jama'a. Za a karrama Farfesa Muyembe-Tamfum daga Jamhuriyar Dimokraɗiyyar Kwango (DRC) ne saboda ƙirƙire-ƙirƙire da jagoranci da kuma bayar da gudunmawar da ta dace wajen kula da kuma magance cututtuka kamar cutar Ebola. Ya taka muhimmiyar rawa wajen inganta sakamakon kiwon lafiya a Jamhuriyar Demokradiyyar Kongo, yankin Afirka, da kuma duniya bakiɗaya. Farfesa Muyembe-Tamfum shi ne Darakta Janar na Cibiyar Nazarin Halittar ta DRC a Kinshasa, cikakken Farfesa a fannin nazarin halittu a Jami'ar Lafiya ta Kinshasa wato Kinshasa Medical School kuma shugaban farko na Kwalejin Kimiyya ta Kongo wato Congolese Academy of Science. Farfesa Muyembe-Tamfum yana cikin tawagar da ta gano cutar na ta Zaâre ebola a lokacin bullar cutar na farko a shekarar 1976, a Lardin Equateur, da ke Tsakiyar DRC.
translations.reference: WHO Director-General announces Global Health Leaders Awards Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot awarded for lifelong commitment to health WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus will present two lifetime achievement awards to recognize outstanding contributions to advancing global health during the high-level opening session of the Seventy-sixth World Health Assembly. The Director-General’s Global Leaders Awards, which were established in 2019, will go to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot. Both recipients are distinguished scientists and public health leaders who were closely involved in the discovery of Ebola virus disease before advancing to leadership positions in global health. The award honours their lifetime achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum from the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) is being recognized for his innovation, leadership and ground-breaking contributions to the management and treatment of diseases such as Ebola virus disease. He has played a critical role in improving health outcomes in the Democratic Republic of the Congo, the African region, and the global community. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director General of the DRC’s National Institute of Biomedical Research in Kinshasa, full Professor of Microbiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School and the inaugural president of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was part of the team that discovered the Zaïre ebolavirus during the first recorded outbreak in 1976, in Equateur Province, Central DRC.
translations.hypothesis: The Director-General of the WHO announced the award of the World Health Organization's Director-General's Special Award to Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot for their contributions to public health. Director-General of the WHO, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, will present the awards at the opening ceremony of the 76th World Health Assembly. The Director-General's Special Award, established in 2019, recognizes Jean-Jacques Muyembe-Tamfum and Peter Piot for their pioneering work in the fight against Ebola. The two laureates are renowned scientists and public health leaders who played a key role in identifying and containing the Ebola outbreak before it reached a global pandemic. The award is a testament to their achievements in public health. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum will be honored from the Democratic Republic of Congo (DRC) for his groundbreaking research and leadership in the fight against diseases such as Ebola. He has made significant contributions to improving health outcomes in the DRC, the African continent, and the world. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum is the Director-General of the National Institute for Medical Research in Kinshasa, a Professor of Epidemiology at the University of Kinshasa Medical School, and the first President of the Congolese Academy of Science. Professor Muyembe-Tamfum was also part of the team that discovered the Zaâre Ebola virus during the first outbreak in 1976 in the Équateur province of the DRC.

translations.source: Daga baya ya taimaka wajen tsara ɗaya daga cikin magunguna masu inganci na farko a kan cutar da tura magungunan rigakafin cutar Ebola na gwaji. Ya yi aiki tuƙuru wajen ƙarfafar Jamhuriyar Dimokaradiyyar Kwango a fannin kimiyya, da kafa cibiyoyin bincike da dama a ƙasar, kuma ya taka muhimmiyar rawa tare da WHO wajen yaƙi da cututtuka masu yaɗuwa. Za a karrama Farfesa Piot daga Beljiyam ne saboda irin jagoranci na musamman da ya yi wajen yaƙar cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki da sauran manyan ƙalubale ga kiwon lafiya a duniya. Ya taka muhimmiyar rawa wajen ƙaddamar da manyan tsare-tsare na kiwon lafiya a duniya kamar UNAIDS, Asusun Duniya, da sauran su waɗanda suka inganta sakamako a fannin kiwon lafiya a duniya. A ƙarkƙashin jagorancinsa, UNAIDS ta zama babbar mai bayar da shawara wajen ɗaukar matakan yaƙi da cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki a duniya, wadda kuma ke jagorantar kawo sauyi a Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya ta hanyar haɗa kan ƙungiyoyin Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya daban-daban guda 10. Farfesa Piot ya kuma taka rawa wajen gano ƙwayar cutar Ebola a farko-farko, da kuma daƙile ɓarkewar cutar ta farko da aka sani, yana shiga cikin binciken cututtuka a zahiri. Ya shugabanci wasu manyan ƙungiyoyi, ya kuma yi aiki a matsayin babban mai ba da shawara, wanda ya haɗa da mai ba da shawara can Covid-19, ya kuma karɓi lambobin yabo da dama. Farfesa Piot shi ne mai ba da shawara na musamman ga Shugaban EC, Farfesa a fannin Lafiyar Duniya, kuma tsohon Darakta a makarantar London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. Darakta Janar na WHO, Dakta Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus ya bayyana cewa waɗannan mutane masu ƙwazo sun nuna jajircewa ta tsawon rayuwa, da wayar da kai babu gajiyawa, jajircewa kan samun daidaito, da kuma sadaukar da kai ga don lafiyar al'umma. "A lokacin da ake fama da matsaloli masu buƙatar kula ta gaggawa da dama, da rikice-rikicen kiwon lafiya, da tashin-tashina, mun fahimci cewa jagoranci a fannin kiwon lafiyar jama'a da kimiyya sun fi kowane lokaci muhimmanci don ciyar da harkar lafiya gaba a faɗin duniya.
translations.reference: He later helped design one of the first effective treatments against the virus and the deployment of experimental Ebola virus disease vaccines. He has worked tirelessly to build the Democratic Republic of the Congo’s scientific capacity, establishing multiple research facilities in the country, and has played key roles with WHO in fighting infectious diseases. Professor Piot from Belgium is being recognized for his extraordinary leadership in the response to the HIV/AIDS epidemic and other significant global health challenges. He has played a vital role in launching major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others that have improved health outcomes around the world. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became the chief advocate for worldwide action against AIDS, also spearheading UN reform by bringing together 10 different UN organizations. Professor Piot also played a part in the earlier discovery of the Ebola virus, and the containment of the first known outbreak, participating in field epidemiological investigations. He has chaired a number of high-level initiatives, served in numerous high-level advisory roles, including on COVID-19, and has received multiple awards. Professor Piot is the Special Advisor to the EC President, a Professor of Global Health and former Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine. “These outstanding individuals embody lifelong dedication, relentless advocacy, a commitment to equity, and selfless service of humanity and people’s health,” said WHO Director-General, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “In a time of multiple emergencies, health crises, and conflict, we recognize that leadership in public health and science are more important than ever to advance global health.
translations.hypothesis: He played a key role in developing one of the first effective treatments for Ebola, working tirelessly to advance public health in the Democratic Republic of Congo. He also established several research centers in the country and collaborated closely with the WHO to combat other infectious diseases. Professor Piot will be honored by Belgium for his exceptional leadership in the fight against HIV/AIDS and other major global health challenges. He was instrumental in the development of major global health initiatives such as UNAIDS, the Global Fund, and others, which have significantly improved health outcomes worldwide. Under his leadership, UNAIDS became a leading voice in the global response to HIV/AIDS, and has also been instrumental in driving change within the World Health Organization by bringing together different UN agencies. Professor Piot was also at the forefront of the early identification of the Ebola virus, and played a key role in the development of diagnostic tests. He has also served as a consultant to several major organizations, and has received numerous awards. Professor Piot is a special advisor to the President of the World Health Organization, and was formerly the Director of the London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine.

translations.source: Waɗanda muke karramawa a yau sun nuna wannan jajircewar. "Labaransu da nasarorin da suka samu sun nuna yadda duniya ta samu ci gaba ta fuskar haɗin gwiwa a matakan duniya, yanki da ƙasa don ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen samar da kulawar gaggawa na lafiya da kuma juriya. Waɗannan masana kimiyya za su ci gaba da jan hankalinmu a kowace rana, ya kuma ƙara da cewa Bikin bayar da lambar yabon ya kasance wani ɓangare na buɗe babban taron World Health Assembly karo na 76 da za a gabatar ranar Lahadi 21 ga watan Mayun 2023 a Geneva.
translations.reference: Our awardees today personify that passion.” “Their combined stories and successes show how much the world has progressed in terms of collaboration at the global, regional and country levels to strengthen health emergency preparedness, response and resilience. These outstanding scientists will continue to inspire us every day,” he added The ceremony for the awards form part of the high-level opening session of the 76th World Health Assembly being held on Sunday 21 May 2023 in Geneva.
translations.hypothesis: Those being honored today exemplify this dedication. "Their achievements and successes demonstrate the progress that can be made through global collaboration at the international, regional, and national levels in promoting health and resilience. These experts will continue to inspire us, and the awarding of the World Health Assembly's 76th session medal will also be a part of the opening ceremony on Sunday, May 21, 2023, in Geneva.

translations.source: WHO ta taya Benin da Mali murnar kawar da cutar trachoma a matsayin cuta mai addabar al'umma WHO ta tabbatar da cewa Benin da Mali sun yi nasarar kawar da trachoma a matsayin cuta mai addabar al'umma, wanda ya sa suka zama ƙasashe na biyar da na shida a yankin da WHO ke kula da shi a Afirka da suka kai wannan matakin. Ƙasashen da a baya suka samu wannan tantancewar ta kawar da cutar trachoma su ne Ghana(Yuni 2018), Gambia (Afrilu 2021), Togo (Mayu 2022) da Malawi (Satumba 2022). Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na ya ce hukumar lafiya ta duniyar tana kuma jinjinawa hukumomin lafiya na Benin da Mali, da kuma ƙungiyoyin haɗin guiwa na ƙasashen waje da na gida bisa wannan nasara da aka samu. Biyo bayan nasarar Benin da Mali, har yanzu trachoma cuta ce mai yaɗuwa a ƙasashen 23 a yankin Afirka da WHO ke lura da shi, wanda hakan ya ƙara kusanta mu muradin da kawar da cutar trachoma da aka tsara don lura da cutatuttukan da ake samu a yankuna masu zafi a shekarar 2021 zuwa 2030. A duniya bakiɗaya, Benin da Mali sun bi sahun wasu ƙasashe 15 da WHO ta tabbatar da cewa sun kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a. Waɗannan ƙasashen su ne Cambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, Jamhuriyar Musulunci ta Iran, Jamhuriyar Dimokuradiyyar Jama'ar Lao, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo da Vanuatu. Kawar da cutar Trachoma ta amfani da dabarun kariya na hukumar lafiya ta duniya wato WHO SAFE strategy. Dukkan ƙasashen na Benin da Mali sun aiwatar da dabarun SAFE da WHO ta ba da shawarar amfani da su don kawar da cutar ta trachoma tare da tallafin WHO da ƙungiyoyin haɗin guiwa. Dabarar SAFE ta ƙunshi tiyata don magance matsalolin trachoma a matakin ƙarshe; maganin rigakafi don warkar da infekshan; tsabtar fuska; da inganta muhalli, musamman inganta samun ruwa da tsaftar muhalli, don rage yaɗuwa.
translations.reference: WHO congratulates Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem WHO has validated Benin and Mali as having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in WHO’s African Region to achieve this significant milestone. Countries that previously received WHO validation for trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022) and Malawi (September 2022). “WHO congratulates the health authorities of Benin and Mali and their network of global and local partners for these milestones”, said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Following Benin’s and Mali’s success, trachoma remains endemic in 23 countries in WHO’s African Region, bringing us a step closer towards the elimination target for trachoma set in the road map for neglected tropical diseases 2021–2030.” Globally, Benin and Mali join 15 other countries that have been validated by WHO for having eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These are Cambodia, China, the Gambia, Ghana, Islamic Republic of Iran, Lao People’s Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo and Vanuatu. Trachoma elimination using WHO’s SAFE strategy Both Benin and Mali implemented the WHO-recommended SAFE strategy to eliminate trachoma with the support of WHO and partners. The SAFE strategy consists of surgery to treat late trachoma complications; antibiotics to clear infection; facial cleanliness; and environmental improvement, particularly improving access to water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.
translations.hypothesis: WHO commends Benin and Mali for eliminating trachoma as a public health problem. The WHO has confirmed that Benin and Mali have successfully eliminated trachoma as a public health problem, making them the fifth and sixth countries in the WHO African region to achieve this milestone. Other countries that have previously achieved trachoma elimination are Ghana (June 2018), Gambia (April 2021), Togo (May 2022), and Malawi (September 2022). Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO, praised the health ministries and partner organizations in Benin and Mali for their efforts in achieving this success. Following the success of Benin and Mali, trachoma remains a public health problem in 23 countries in the WHO African region, highlighting the need to continue efforts to eliminate trachoma as part of the WHO's strategy to address infectious diseases in low-income countries between 2021 and 2030. Globally, Benin and Mali are among the 15 countries that the WHO has confirmed have eliminated trachoma as a public health problem. These countries are Cambodia, China, Gambia, Ghana, the Islamic Republic of Iran, the Lao People's Democratic Republic, Malawi, Mexico, Morocco, Myanmar, Nepal, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Togo, and Vanuatu. The elimination of trachoma has been achieved through the WHO's SAFE strategy. All of the Benin and Mali countries have implemented the SAFE strategy, which includes surgery for severe cases, antibiotic treatment for infections, facial cleanliness, and environmental improvements, particularly access to clean water and sanitation, to reduce transmission.

translations.source: Ta hanyar International Trachoma Initiative ta ƙasa da ƙasa, kamfanin Pfizer ya ba da gudummuwar maganin azithromycin don aiwatar da tsarin na SAFE. Ƙasar Benin ta haɗa dabarun kawar da cutar ta trachoma tare da waɗanda aka aiwatar kan wasu cututtuka na wurare masu zafi da ba a kula da su (NTDs), a ƙarƙashin inuwar Hukumar Kula da Cututtuka masu Yaɗuwa ta Ƙasa. Trachoma ita ce cutar wurare masu zafi da ba kula da su (NTD) ta uku da aka kawar da ita a Benin, bayancutar dracunculiasis (a shekarar 2009) da cutar gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (a shekarar 2021). Ƙasar Mali ta gudanar da binciken kwakwaf game da tasiru da sa ido tare da ƙaddamar da matakan shawo kan matsalar, duk da ƙalubalen tsaro a yankunan arewacin ƙasar da kuma tashe-tashen hankula a tsakanin al'umma a 'yan shekarun nan. Cutar ta Trachoma ita ce ta cutar wurare masu zafi da ba kula da su (NTD) ta farko da aka kawar a ƙasar Mali, don haka yanzu ta shiga cikin rukunin ƙasashe 47 na duniya waɗanda suka kawar da aƙalla cutar wurare masu zafi da ba kula da su (NTD) guda ɗaya. "Waɗannan nasarori ne masu ban sha'awa a fannin kiwon lafiyar jama'a, in ji Dr Ibrahima Soce Fall, Daraktan shirin na WHO Global NTD. "Benin da Mali sun nuna yadda jajircewa a gwamnatance, haɗin kan ɓangarori daban-daban, sa ido da kuma haɗa kan al'umma ke aiki tare don cimma nasarar kawar da cututtuka." An samu gagarumin ci gaba a yaƙi da cutar ta trachoma a cikin 'yan shekarun da suka gabata. Adadin mutanen da ke buƙatar maganin cutar ta trachoma a yankin da WHO ke kula da shi a Afirka ya ragu da miliyan 84, daga miliyan 189 a shekarar 2014 zuwa miliyan 105 a watan Yunin 2022. Tsokaci ga Edita: Trachoma har yanzu matsalar lafiyar jama'a a cikin ƙasashe 41 (a watan Yuni 2022) inda kimanin mutane miliyan 125 da ke zaune a yankunan ke buƙatar yin rigakafin cutar.
translations.reference: Through the International Trachoma Initiative, the antibiotic azithromycin is donated by Pfizer to elimination programmes implementing the SAFE strategy. Benin has integrated trachoma elimination interventions with those implemented against other neglected tropical diseases (NTDs), under the umbrella of the National Programme for Communicable Diseases. Trachoma is the third NTD to be eliminated in Benin, after dracunculiasis (in 2009) and gambiense human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted trachoma impact and surveillance surveys and rolled out interventions to achieve elimination targets, despite security challenges in the northern regions of the country and sociopolitical upheavals in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, which therefore now joins a global group of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD. “These are impressive public health achievements,” said Dr Ibrahima Socé Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. “Benin and Mali demonstrate how strong political will, cross-sector integration, surveillance and community engagement can work in concert to achieve disease elimination.” Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma over the past few years. The number of people requiring antibiotic treatment for trachoma in the WHO African Region fell by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million as of June 2022. Note to the editor: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022) with an estimated 125 million people living in areas requiring interventions against the disease.
translations.hypothesis: Through its global partnership with the International Trachoma Initiative, Pfizer has provided azithromycin to support the implementation of the SAFE program. Benin has implemented trachoma control programs in conjunction with other neglected tropical disease (NTD) control efforts, under the leadership of the National Programme for the Control of Tropical Diseases. Trachoma is one of the leading neglected tropical diseases (NTDs) in Benin, along with onchocerciasis (in 2009) and human African trypanosomiasis (in 2021). Mali has conducted a series of surveys and interventions to address the problem, despite security challenges in the northern regions and fluctuating levels of community engagement in recent years. Trachoma is the first NTD to be eliminated in Mali, and the country is now part of the global network of 47 countries that have eliminated at least one NTD. "These are remarkable achievements in public health," said Dr. Ibrahima Soce Fall, Director of the WHO Global NTD Programme. "Benin and Mali demonstrate the importance of government commitment, multisectoral collaboration, surveillance, and community engagement in achieving elimination." Significant progress has been made in the fight against trachoma in recent years. The number of people in the WHO African region who need treatment for trachoma has decreased by 84 million, from 189 million in 2014 to 105 million in June 2022. Note: Trachoma remains a public health problem in 41 countries (as of June 2022), with an estimated 125 million people living in trachoma-endemic areas who need vision correction.

translations.source: Ana samun cutar ta Trachoma ne musamman a yankunan karkara mafi talauci a Afirka, Tsakiya da Kudancin Amurka, Asiya, Yammacin Pacific da Gabas ta Tsakiya. Yankin Afirka da WHO ke lura da shi yana fama da cutar ta trachoma, inda mutane miliyan 105 ke zaune a yankunan da ke fuskantar haɗarin, wanda ke wakiltar kashi 84% adadin masu cutar ta trachoma a duniya. Trachoma ita ce cutar da ke kan gaba wajen haifar da makanta a duniya. Cutar na samuwa ne sakamakon kamuwa da ƙwayar cutar Chlamydia trachomatis. Cutar tana yaɗuwa tsakanin mutane ne ta hanyar ta gurɓatattun hannaye, kayan sawa da na amfani da ƙudaje waɗanda suka yi mu'amala ruwan da ke fita daga idanu ko hancin mai cutar. Ababen hatsari da ke tattare da muhalli da ke haddasa yaɗuwar cutar ta trachoma sun haɗa da rashin tsafta, cunkoso a gidaje, rashin isasshen ruwa ko wuraren amfani masu tsafta. A shekara 1996, WHO ta ƙaddamar da Gidauniyar Haɗaka don kawar da Trachoma ta Duniya nan da shekara ta 2020 (GET2020). Tare da sauran abokan tarayya a cikin Gidauniyar ta haɗaka, WHO ba da goyon baya wajen aiwatar da dabarun SAFE da kuma ƙarfafa yunƙurin ƙasa ta hanyar tantance cututtuka, lura da sa ido, tantance ayyuka da tattara kayan aiki da ke taimakawa wajen kawar da cutar ta trachoma a matsayin matsalar lafiyar jama'a. Tsarin gudanarwar, wanda Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya ta amince da shi a shekarar 2020 ta hanyar shawara mai lamba 73 (33) da ka yanke, ta sanya 2030 a matsayin sabon lokacin da ake nufin kawar da cutar a faɗin duniya.
translations.reference: Trachoma is found mainly in the poorest and most rural areas of Africa, Central and South America, Asia, the Western Pacific and the Middle East. The WHO African Region is disproportionately affected by trachoma with 105 million people living in at-risk areas, which represents 84% of the global trachoma burden. Trachoma is the leading infectious cause of blindness worldwide. The disease is caused by infection with the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. Infection is transmitted from person to person through contaminated fingers, fomites and flies that have come into contact with discharge from the eyes or nose of an infected person. Environmental risk factors for trachoma transmission include poor hygiene, overcrowded households, inadequate access to water or use of proper sanitation facilities. In 1996, WHO launched the WHO Alliance for the Global Elimination of Trachoma by the year 2020 (GET2020). With other partners in the Alliance, WHO supports country implementation of the SAFE strategy and strengthening of national capacity through epidemiological assessment, monitoring, surveillance, project evaluation and resource mobilization contributing towards elimination of trachoma as a public health problem. The road map, endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through its decision 73(33), sets 2030 as the new target date for global elimination.
translations.hypothesis: Trachoma is a contagious eye infection that is most common in arid and semi-arid regions of Africa, the Middle East, the Americas, Asia, and the Pacific Islands. The African region, which is being monitored by the WHO, is particularly affected by trachoma, with over 105 million people living in areas at risk, representing 84% of the global trachoma population. Trachoma is caused by the bacterium Chlamydia trachomatis. It is spread from person to person through close contact, such as touching contaminated objects or sharing towels with someone who has infected eyes. Environmental factors that contribute to the spread of trachoma include poor sanitation, overcrowding, lack of access to clean water, and inadequate hygiene. In 1996, the WHO launched the Global Trachoma Elimination Program (GET), which aims to eliminate trachoma worldwide by 2020. As part of GET, the WHO works with other partners to implement SAFE strategies, which involve surgery, antibiotics, facial cleanliness, and environmental improvement. The WHO's strategy, which was endorsed by the World Health Assembly in 2020 through resolution 73.33, sets 2030 as the new target date for global trachoma elimination.

translations.source: Jarirai miliyan 152 ne aka haifa kafin lokacin haihuwarsu ya yi. 1. A cikin kowane jarirai 10 da ake haifa, 1 bakwaini ne - kuma kowane sakan 40, 1 na waɗannan jariran yana mutuwa. Yawan jariran da ake haifa kafin lokacin haihuwarsu ya yi bai sauya ba a cikin shekaru goma da suka wuce a duk sassa na duniya. Tasirin rikice-rikice da na sauyin yanayi da ma na cutar Covid-19 duk ƙarin ƙalubale ne ga mata da jarirai a ko'ina. Akwai buƙatar ɗaukar matakan gaggawa domin dakatar da haihuwar jarirai kafin lokaci ya yi tare da samar da cikakkiyar kulawa ga jariran tare da iyalan da hakan ya auku gare su. Bisa ƙiyasi, jarirai miliyan 13.4 aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata a shekarar 2020 kuma kusan su miliyan 1 daga cikinsu suka rasu a saboda hakan, a cewar wani rahoton da ma'aikatun Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya suka fitar a kwanan nan. Wannan adadi daidai yake da 1 cikin jararai 10 da aka haifa da wuri (kafin mako 37 da ɗaukar ciki) a duniya baki ɗaya. Daftarin Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth wanda Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta samar da haɗin guiwar Hukumar Kula da Ƙananan Yara ta Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya wato UNICEF tare da PMNCH wadda ita ce haɗaka mafi girma tsakanin mata da yara da kuma matasa a duk faɗin duniya ya abin da ke buƙatar ɗaukin gaggawa da ba san da shi ba na haihuwar jarirai kafin lokacin da ya kamata, wanda ba a fahimci yawansa da tsananinsa ba, wanda kuma yana dakushe cigaba da ma rayuwar jarirai. Rahoton ya ƙunshi bayanai da aka samo daga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da ta UNICEF wanda makarantar kula da tsafta da cutuka a yankuna masu zafi ta Landan ta tsara a kan yawaitar jariran da ake haihuwa kafin lokacin da ya kamata. A gaba ɗaya, ta gano cewa yawan jariran da ake haifa kafin lokaci bai sauya ba a kowane yanki na duniya a cikin shekaru goma da suka wuce, inda aka samu jarirai miliyan 152 da aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata tsakanin shekarar 2010 zuwa ta 2020.
translations.reference: 152 million babies born preterm in the last decade Of every 10 babies born, 1 is preterm – and every 40 seconds, 1 of those babies dies Preterm birth rates have not changed in the past decade in any region of the world The impacts of conflict, climate change, COVID-19 are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere Action is needed urgently to improve the prevention of preterm birth, alongside better care for affected babies and their families An estimated 13.4 million babies were born pre-term in 2020, with nearly 1 million dying from preterm complications, according to a new report released by United Nations agencies and partners today. This is equivalent to around 1 in 10 babies born early (before 37 weeks of pregnancy) worldwide. Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth, produced by the World Health Organization (WHO) and the United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF) together with PMNCH -- the world’s largest alliance for women, children, and adolescents, sounds the alarm on a “silent emergency” of preterm birth, long under-recognized in its scale and severity, which is impeding progress in improving children’s health and survival. The report includes updated estimates from WHO and UNICEF, prepared with the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine, on the prevalence of preterm births. Overall, it finds that preterm birth rates have not changed in any region in the world in the past decade, with 152 million vulnerable babies born too soon from 2010 to 2020.
translations.hypothesis: Approximately 152,000 babies are born prematurely each year worldwide. In every 10 babies born, 1 is born prematurely – and of those, about 40% die. The number of babies born prematurely has not changed significantly in the past decade in any part of the world. However, the increasing incidence of preterm birth is a major public health concern, particularly in low- and middle-income countries. There is an urgent need to take action to reduce the incidence of preterm birth and to improve the care of preterm babies and their families. In 2020, an estimated 13.4 million babies were born prematurely, with about 1 million dying as a result. This represents 1 in 10 babies born prematurely worldwide. The "Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth" initiative, led by the World Health Organization (WHO) and UNICEF, aims to reduce the incidence of preterm birth and to improve the care of preterm babies and their families. The initiative is based on the findings of a comprehensive review of the literature on preterm birth, which was conducted by the London School of Hygiene and Tropical Medicine. The review found that the incidence of preterm birth has not changed significantly in the past decade, with an estimated 152,000 babies being born prematurely each year worldwide.

translations.source: Haihuwar jarirai kafin lokacin da ya kamata shi ne yanzu ya fi haddasa mace-macen yara, inda yake haddasa mutuwar yaro guda duk cikin su 5 da suke rasu kafin su shekara 5. Jariran da aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata sukan fuskanci ƙalubale na tsawon rayuwa, inda sukan iya samun wata naƙasa ko kuma rashin girma yadda ya kamata. Bambancin rayuwa dangane da yanki ko matsayin tattalin arziƙi ko kuma launin fata. La'akari da wani shahararren rahoto game da lamarin a shekara ta 2012, wannan daftarin na Born too soon ya fayyace yadda ake samuwar jarirai bakwani (waɗanda aka haifa kafin lokacin da ya kamata) dalla-dalla tare da tasirin da hakan yake da shi a kan mata da iyalai da al'umma da kuma tattalin arziƙi. A yawancin lokuta, inda aka haifi jarirai shi yake tasiri wajen rayuwarsu ko rashinta. Rahoton ya yi nuni da cewa, jariri 1 ne kawai a cikin 10 ke rayuwa idan an haife shi a lokacin da ko kusa lokacin haihuwarsa bai yi ba (ƙasa da mako 28 da ɗaukar ciki) a ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi, saɓanin a ƙasashe masu arziƙi inda adadin ya kai jararai 9 a cikin 10. Cike giɓin rashin daidaiton da ake samu ta fuskar launin fata da ƙabila da tattalin arziƙi da ma samun hanyoyin kiwon lafiya nagartattu shi zai taimaka a rage samun jarirai bakwaini da rage mace-macensu da kuma samun naƙasarsu hatta a ƙasashe masu yalwar arziƙi. Kudancin nahiyar Asiya da yankin Afirka kudu da Sahara su suka fi yawan jarirai bakwaini sannan kuma a nan ne aka fi samun mutuwarsu. Waɗannan yankuna guda biyu idan aka tara su sun kwashe kashi 65 a cikin ɗari na yawan jarirai bakwaini na duk faɗin duniya. Har'ilayau, rahoton ya bayyana cewa tasirin rikice-rikice da na sauyin yanayi da gurɓacewar muhalli da na cutar Covid-19 dakuma tsadar rayuwar barazan ce ga mata da jarirai a ko'ina.
translations.reference: Preterm birth is now the leading cause of child deaths, accounting for more than 1 in 5 of all deaths of children occurring before their 5th birthday. Preterm survivors can face lifelong health consequences, with an increased likelihood of disability and developmental delays. Survival gaps by region, income, race Building from a landmark report on the topic in 2012, this new Born too soon “decade” report provides a comprehensive overview of the prevalence of preterm birth and its profound impact on women, families, societies and economies. Too often, where babies are born determines if they survive. The report notes that only 1 in 10 extremely preterm babies (<28 weeks) survive in low-income countries, compared to more than 9 in 10 in high-income countries. Gaping inequalities related to race, ethnicity, income, and access to quality care determine the likelihood of preterm birth, death, and disability, even in high-income countries. Southern Asia and sub-Saharan Africa have the highest rates of preterm birth, and preterm babies in these regions face the highest mortality risk. Together, these two regions account for more than 65% of preterm births globally. The report also highlights that the impacts of conflict, climate change and environmental damage, COVID-19, and rising living costs are increasing risks for women and babies everywhere.
translations.hypothesis:
The premature birth of babies is a significant problem, as it can lead to a higher risk of death for both the child and the mother. Babies born too early often face a number of challenges, including underdeveloped organs and a weakened immune system. These challenges can lead to a variety of health problems, such as respiratory distress, feeding difficulties, and infections.

According to a recent report, premature babies are more likely to die than full-term babies, particularly in low-income countries. The report found that only one in ten premature babies survive to adulthood, compared to nine in ten full-term babies.

The report also found that there is a correlation between the race of the mother and the risk of premature birth. Black women are more likely to give birth prematurely than white women, and they are also more likely to die as a result of premature birth.

The report concludes that there are a number of factors that contribute to the problem of premature birth, including poverty, lack of access to healthcare, and environmental factors. The report recommends a number of interventions to reduce the incidence of premature birth, including improving access to prenatal care, promoting healthy lifestyles, and addressing environmental hazards.

The problem of premature birth is a global issue, with the highest rates in low-income countries. The World Health Organization estimates that 15 million babies are born prematurely each year, and that 1 million of these babies die as a result.

translations.source: Misali, an ƙiyasta cewa gurɓatacciyar iska kawai tana haddasa haihuwar jarirai bakaini miliyan 6 a duk shekara. A wani sabon nazari na rahoton an fahimci cewa, kusan bakaini 1 a duk su 10 da aka haifa za a iske yana a cikin ƙasashen da yawan rikice-rikice ya fi addabar su ne. Matsalolin laulayin ciki da suke samuwa sakamakon ɗaukar ciki da wuri ga budurwa ko hawan jinin da ya samu sanadiyar juna-biyu suna da dangantaka sosai da haihuwar bakwaini. Wannan ya sa akwai buƙatar a inganta kula da lafiya ta fuskar zamantakewar aure da samun haihuwa, ciki har da tazarar haihuwa da samar da cikakkiyar kulawa ga mata masu ciki da kuma lokacin haihuwarsu. Matakan da za a ɗauka:ƙasashe su ƙara zuba jari sannan iyaye su kuma su fito domin yin gwagwarmaya a kan lamarin. A shekaru goma da suka gabata, an kuma samu cigaba ta fuskar gwagwarmayar al'umma kan rage haihuwar bakwaini da yin ɓarin ciki, inda iyaye da ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da manyan makarantu da ƙungiyoyi masu fafutuka da sauransu suka shiga tsundum aka dama da su. A duk faɗin duniya, ƙungiyoyin iyalai na waɗanda suka haifi bakwaini a baya su ne a kan gaba wajen yin kira da a inganta kula tare da sanya tsare-tsare sannan kuma da taimaka wa wasu iyalan da hakan ya auku gare su. Har zuwa lokacin da aka gudanar d Taron Ƙara wa Juna Ilimi a Kan Kula da Jarirai da aka yi a Cape Town ta Afirka ta Kudu tsakanin 8-11 ga watan Mayu, Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da ta UNICEF da ta UNFPA da ku ta PMNCH duk sun yi ta kiraye-kiraye kan inganta lafiyar mata da ta jarirai da kuma lalubo hanyoyin daƙile haihuwar bakwaini kamar haka: Ƙara zuba jari: Samar da kuɗaɗe daga ciki da wajen ƙasa domin inganta lafiyar mata masu ciki da jarirai ta hanyar tabbatar da an bayar da kulawar da ta dace a wurare mabuƙata kuma a lokacin da ya dace. Gaggauta aiwatarwa: Cimma burin ƙasa na cigaba ta hanyar aiwatar da tsare-tsaren da aka yi na inganta lafiyar mata masu ciki da jarirai.
translations.reference: For example, air pollution is estimated to contribute to 6 million preterm births each year. Nearly 1 in 10 preterm babies are born in the 10 most fragile countries affected by humanitarian crises, according to a new analysis in the report. Maternal health risks, such as adolescent pregnancy and pre-eclampsia, are closely linked to preterm births. This underlines the need to ensure access to sexual and reproductive health services, including effective family planning, with high-quality care in pregnancy and around the time of birth. Agenda for action: more country investment and parent-led activism The past decade has also seen a growth of community activism on preterm birth and stillbirth prevention, driven by networks of parents, health professionals, academia, civil society, and others. Throughout the world, groups for affected families of preterm birth have been at the forefront of advocating for access to better care and policy change and supporting other families. Leading up to the International Maternal Newborn Health Conference, in Cape Town, South Africa from 8-11 May, WHO, UNICEF, UNFPA and PMNCH are calling for the following actions to improve care for women and newborns and mitigate risks from preterm births: Increased investments: Mobilizing international and domestic resources to optimize maternal and newborn health, ensuring high quality care is provided when and where it is needed. Accelerated implementation: Meeting country targets for progress through implementing established national policies for maternal and newborn care.
translations.hypothesis: For example, it is estimated that unsafe abortions cause approximately 6 million unintended pregnancies each year. A recent study found that nearly 1 in 10 women who attempt to have abortions in countries where they are illegal do so in unsafe conditions. Complications from unsafe abortions, such as infection, hemorrhage, and injury, are a major cause of maternal morbidity and mortality. This highlights the need to improve access to safe and legal abortion services, as well as to provide comprehensive care for pregnant women and their newborns. Steps that can be taken include: increasing investment in reproductive health services; strengthening national policies and laws to ensure access to safe abortion; and providing training and resources for healthcare providers. In recent years, there has been a growing movement to reduce unsafe abortion rates and to promote access to safe abortion services. This has been driven by a number of factors, including the work of civil society organizations, healthcare providers, and other stakeholders. Across the world, women who have had unsafe abortions are calling for better access to safe abortion services. At the International Conference on Safe Abortion in Cape Town, South Africa, in May 2013, representatives from the World Health Organization (WHO), UNICEF, UNFPA, and other organizations called for increased investment in reproductive health services, as well as for the strengthening of national policies and laws to ensure access to safe abortion.

translations.source: Ƙarfafa dukkan ɓangarori: Tallafa wa ilimi a dukkan matakan rayuwa, samar da tallafi ta fuskar tattalin arziƙi a dukkan fannoni, ƙarfafa wa jama'a fuskantar sauyin yanayi sannan kuma da ƙarfafa tare-tsaren taimakon gaggawa. Samar da ƙirƙire-ƙirƙiren cikin gida: Zuba jari a ƙirƙire-ƙirƙire da bincike na cikin gida domin taimaka wa cigaba ta fuskar inganta tare da daidaita samar da kulawa gare su. Tsakure: Farfesa Joy Lawn, Makarantar Kula da Tsafta da Samar da Magunguna ga Ƙasashe masu Yanayin Zafi ta Landan. Jagorantar Daftarin Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023)da na Born too soon (2012). "Wannan sabon rahoton ya nuna cewa rashin ɗaukar matakan da suka dace ya haddasa haiuwar jarirai bakwaini har miliyan 152. Duk da yake wasu yankuna sun fi fuskantar matsalar, amma haihuwar yara bakwaini ƙalubale ne ga kiwon lafiya a kowace ƙasa. Ƙarin zuba jari a kan kula da jarirai zai taimaka wajen tsare iyalai da dama daga damuwa. Akwai kuma buƙatar yin aiki tuƙuru domin hana haihuwar jarirai bakwaini wanda hakan kuma zai taimaka wajen rage yin ɓari da mutuwar mata masu ciki. Waɗannan tagwayen hanyoyi na hana haihuwar jarirai bakwaini idan suka haɗu, za su taimaka wajen samar da lafiyayyun mutane da al'ummomi waɗanda za su kawo wa ƙasa cigaba ta fuskar tattalin arziƙi da zamantakewa. Ya'ya da jikokinmu sun dogara da abin da muka yi a yau --- jarin da za mu saka ko da ba mai yawa ba ne zai iya zama mai yawa ga kowace ƙasa.
translations.reference: Integration across sectors: Promoting education through the life cycle; supporting smarter economic investments, with co-financing across sectors; strengthening climate adaptation responses across the life-course; and advancing the coordination and resilience of emergency systems. Locally driven innovation: Investing in locally led innovation and research to support improvements in quality of care and equity in access. Quotes: Prof. Joy Lawn, London School of Hygiene & Tropical Medicine Co-lead, Born too soon: decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012) "This new report shows that the cost of inaction over the last decade was 152 million babies born too soon. While some regions are more affected, preterm birth threatens health progress in every country. Greater investment in the care of vulnerable newborns can save millions of families from heartbreak. More work is also needed to prevent preterm birth, which will also improve progress in reducing stillbirths and maternal deaths. Together these twin tracks of preterm prevention and care will produce healthier individuals and societies to deliver on economic and social development. Our next generation depends on us all acting now -- the investment may not be small but the returns on this investment will be major for every country"
translations.hypothesis: Empowering all sectors: Promote education at all levels, provide financial support in all fields, encourage communities to address climate change, and foster disaster preparedness. Fostering innovation: Invest in domestic innovation and research to promote sustainable development and improve the quality of life. Advocacy: Professor Joy Lawn, School of Public Health and Primary Care at the University of London. Leading the Born too soon initiative: a decade of action on preterm birth (2023) and Born too soon (2012). "This new report shows that inadequate care contributes to the deaths of over 1.5 million newborns each year. While the problem is more acute in some regions, preterm birth is a major challenge for healthcare systems worldwide. Increased investment in newborn care can save countless lives. There is also a need for urgent action to prevent preterm birth, which would also help to reduce maternal mortality. By working together to address this issue, we can create healthier populations and stronger economies. The health and well-being of our children depend on the choices we make today. Investing in their future is an investment in the future of our world.

translations.source: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Ɗaya daga cikin marubuta na Born Too Soon sannan kuma jagora na Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) na ƙasa. A yanzu kenan babu dalili na yin shiru. Yanzu muna da wuƙa da nama da ilimi na sauya makomar jariranmu da wannan abin zai iya shafa. Ina mai alfahari da ganin a yanzu gwamnatin ƙasata ta mayar da hankali a kan aiwatarwa hatta a yankunan karkara. Shekaru goma masu zuwa suna iya zama, kai dole ne ma su zama daban dangane da masu fuskantar haihuwar bakwaini. Dr Anshu Banerjee, Darektan kula da lafiyar mata masu ciki da jarirai da yara da matasa da harkar da ta shafi tsufa na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO). Tabbatar da an samar da ingantacciyar kulawa ga waɗannan mitsitsan jarirai da iyalansu ya zama wajibi domin inganta kula da yara da rage mace-macensu. Akwai buƙatar samun cigaba ta fuskar kula da jarirai bakwaini --- wannan na nufin cewa dole kowace mace mai ciki ta iya samun damar halartar asibiti kafin da kuma yayin da take ɗauke da juna biyu domin gano tare da fuskantar ƙalubale. Steven Lauwerier, Darektan Kiwon Lafiya (a.i.), UNICEF Babban rashi da damuwa ne ke biyo bayan dukkan mutuwar jariri bakwaini.
translations.reference: Dr Nahya Salim, Tanzania: Born Too Soon co-author and national lead of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) “There is now no excuse for silence. We now have in our hands the devices and the knowledge to change outcomes for our most vulnerable newborns. I am proud to see my government now investing and committed to implementation across even rural areas. The next decade can and must be different for those facing preterm birth here and everywhere.” Dr Anshu Banerjee, Director for Maternal, Newborn, Child and Adolescent Health and Ageing at WHO “Ensuring quality care for these tiniest, most vulnerable babies and their families is absolutely imperative for improving child health and survival. Progress is also needed to help prevent preterm births -- this means every woman must be able to access quality health services before and during pregnancy to identify and manage risks.” Steven Lauwerier, Director of Health (a.i.), UNICEF “After every preterm death is a trail of loss and heartbreak.
translations.hypothesis: Dr. Nahya Salim, Tanzania: One of the leading experts on premature birth and the director of Newborn Essential Solutions and Technologies 360 (NEST360) in the country. Now is the time to speak up. We have the knowledge and the tools to change the lives of these vulnerable babies. I am encouraged to see that the government is now prioritizing the care of premature babies, even in remote areas. The next ten years could be transformative, but only if we continue to work together. Dr. Anshu Banerjee, Director of Maternal, Newborn, and Child Health and a member of the Executive Board of the World Health Organization (WHO). Ensuring that all mothers and babies receive quality care is essential for improving child survival and reducing maternal mortality. There is a need for increased investment in newborn care – this means ensuring that every mother has access to a safe place to give birth and receive postnatal care. Steven Lauwerier, Acting Director of Health, UNICEF. Premature birth is a major public health problem.

translations.source: Duk da cigaba da aka samu a duniya ta fuskoki da dama, babu wata cigaba ta a-zo-a-gani da aka samu dangane da yawan jarirai bakwaini da kuma rage mace-macensu. Yawan yana razanarawa. Lokaci ya yi na inganta lafiyar mata masu ciki da kuma jarirai bakwaini domin tabbatar da samun ingantacciyar lafiya da kyakkyawar rayuwa gare su. Helga Fogstad, Babban Darekta na PMNCH Daftarin Born too soon ya nuna wajibcin ƙara zuba jari da sa-ido yadda ya kamata a kan haihuwar jarirai bakwaini wanda shi ne babban dalilin mutuwar yara 'yan ƙasa da shekara biyar. Cigaban da ake samu dangane da kiwon lafiyar mata masu ciki da na jarirai da ma samuwar ɓarin ciki na mai haƙin rijiya ne, ga shi kuma ya ƙara samun koma-baya sakamakon cutar Covid-19 da sauyin yanayi da ƙaruwar rikice-rikice da kuma tsadar rayuwa. Idan muka haɗa kai, gwamnatocinmu da masu hannu da shuni da ma'aikatu masu zaman kansu da ƙungiyoyi masu fafutuka da iyaye da kuma ma'aikatan lafiya, za mu iya fito da wannan abin da ke buƙatar gaggawa har ya zama cikin muhimman abin da ɓangaren kiwon lafiya na ƙasa ya sa a gaba ta yadda za a gina al'umma ta hanyar tallafa wa iyalai da ƙungiyoyi da ma ɓangarori na tattalin arziƙi a ko'ina. "Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: iyaye ga Jalen Foster, kuma waɗanda suka kafa Jalen's Gift Foundation An kafa gidauniyarmu ne bayan an da muka rasa ɗanmu mai suna Jalen Foster wanda bakwaini ne da aka haifa tun cikinsa yana mako 27 sakamakon harbuwar da ya yi da ƙwayar cuta mai suna streptococous nau'I na B. Rashin ɗan namu ya kasance babban tashin hankali a gare mu kuma babu wanda a tallafa mana daga cikin al'umma, don haka muka ga zai dace a faranta wa iyalan da suka rasa ɗa.
translations.reference: Despite the many advances the world has made in the past decade, we have made no progress in reducing the number of small babies born too soon or averting the risk of their death. The toll is devasting. It’s time we improve access to care for pregnant mothers and preterm infants and ensure every child gets a healthy start and thrives in life.” Helga Fogstad, Executive Director, PMNCH “Born too soon underlines why we must increase investment and accountability for preterm birth – the world’s largest cause of death of children under the age of five. Progress is flatlining for maternal and newborn health, as well as the prevention of stillbirths, and is now pushed back further through the devastating combination of COVID-19, climate change, expanding conflicts and rising living costs. “By working together in partnership – governments, donors, the private sector, civil society, parents, and health professionals – we can sound the alarm about this “silent emergency”, and bring preterm prevention and care efforts to the forefront of national health and development efforts, building human capital by supporting families, societies and economies everywhere. ”Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, and founders of Jalen’s Gift Foundation “Our foundation was founded after the loss of our son, Jalen Foster, who was a preterm stillbirth at 27 weeks due to prenatal onset of a bacteria known as group B streptococcus. Losing our son was devastating, and there was no support in the community and that’s when we decided to provide a place of comfort and hope for families who have lost a baby.
translations.hypothesis: Despite progress in many areas, there has been little improvement in the rates of maternal mortality and neonatal deaths. This is a major concern. It is time to focus on improving the health of mothers and newborns in order to ensure that they have the opportunity to live healthy and fulfilling lives. Gabriela Fogstad, Director of the PMNCH Born too soon project, highlights the urgent need for increased investment in neonatal care, which is a major cause of infant mortality under the age of five. Progress in maternal and neonatal health has been hampered by a number of factors, including poverty, lack of access to healthcare, and the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and climate change. If we work together, governments, healthcare providers, non-profit organizations, and families can make a real difference. "Gabriela and Jerome Foster, Las Vegas, United States: parents of Jalen Foster, who died at the age of 27 from complications of a bacterial infection. They founded Jalen's Gift Foundation after their son's death to support other families who have lost a child.

translations.source: Ba mu son sauran iyalai su ji irin abin da muka ji. Don wannan ne muka kafa Gidauniyar Jalena don taimaka wa sauran iyalan da suke fuskantar rashi don samar ayyuka kyauta a gare su kamar hotuna da tallafin rashi da kuma taimakawa ta yadda za a tuna wanda aka rasa. Sauke manhajar labarai ta Born too soon
translations.reference: We didn’t want other families to feel like we did – alone. So, we started Jalen’s Gift Foundation to help other families going through a loss, to provide multiple free services, such as photograph, grief support and help towards memorial services.” ***Download the Born too soon media resources
translations.hypothesis: We don't want other families to experience the same hardships we have faced. That's why we established the Jalena Foundation to help other families facing financial difficulties by providing them with free services such as photography and financial assistance, as well as helping to honor and remember loved ones who have passed away. Please download the "Born too soon" news app.

translations.source: Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya sun kai wani jirgin sama mai dauke da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya zuwa Sudan Haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) sun kai wani jirgin sama mai ɗauke da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya zuwa ƙasar Sudan. wani jirgi ɗauke da kayayyakin jinya da tiyatar gaggawa da muhimman magunguna sun isa filin jirgin saman port Sudan da safiyar yau. kayan waɗanda darajar su ta kai dalar Amurka 444 000, shine na farko da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta iya kaiwa ta jirgin sama zuwa Sudan tun bayan ɓarkewar rikicin. Hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta rarraba kayayyakin kowon lafiya kafin ƙaruwar rikicin. Waɗancan sun ƙare ne ƴan kwanaki kaɗan bayan an bada adadin waɗanda suka jikkata. Aikewa da jirgin na zuwa ne a wani mataki na cigaba da bada agajin da haɗaɗaɗiyya daular larabawa(UAE) ke yi na bada tallafawa wa al’ummar Sudan da ke nuna ƙarfafa dangatakar ta da ƙasar Sudan, musamman ma ta la’akari da halin da ƙasar ke ciki a halin yanzu. Har ila yau faɗaɗa ayyukan jinƙai na haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa tare da jadadda alaƙar ta da sauran ƙasashe dangane da ƴan uwantaka da jajircewarta na taimakon wasu a lokutan rikici da halin gaggawa. Ƙaramar ministar haɗin gwiwar ƙasa da ƙasa Dakta Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy tace haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa (UAE) na cigaba da yin aiki kafaɗa da kafaɗa da hukumar lafiya ta duniya domin tabbatar da nasarar tura muhimman ayyuka da dabarun ayyukan agajin gaggawa da magunguna da abinci don taimakawa wajen magance halin da ake ciki a yanzu na rikicin Sudan. A daidai lokacin da ake kira ga dukkan rikice rikice da juna da su gaggauta tsagaita wuta tare da kawar da matsalar da ke janyo wa al'ummar Sudan wahalhalun da ba za a iya mantawa da su ba, Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa ta tsaya tsayin daka wajen bayar da taimako da taimako ga kasashe a lokutan bukata.
translations.reference: UAE and WHO deliver air lift of critical medical supplies to Sudan The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization have delivered 30 tons of urgent medical supplies to Sudan today. A plane carrying supplies for injury treatment, emergency surgeries, and essential drugs arrived in Port Sudan Airport early this morning. The shipment, valued at US$ 444 000, is the first that WHO has been able to deliver by air to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. WHO distributed supplies to heath facilities prior to the escalation of conflict. These were exhausted after a few days given the number of injured. The dispatch of the aircraft comes as part of the UAE's continuous relief efforts in support of the Sudanese people and reflects its deep relations with Sudan, particularly in light of the current situation the country is facing. The relief is also an extension of the UAE’s humanitarian vision and underscores its relations with other countries based on human fraternity and its commitment to assisting others during crises and emergency situations. Dr Reem bint Ebrahim Al Hashimy, Minister of State for International Cooperation, said that the UAE "continues to work closely alongside the World Health Organization to ensure the successful deployment of the critical logistics operations to send urgent medical and food aid to help address the current crisis in Sudan. In parallel with calls for all sides of the current conflict to immediately cease hostilities and de-escalate a situation that is causing untold suffering for the Sudanese people, the UAE is steadfast in its commitment to providing aid and assistance to countries in times of need.
translations.hypothesis: The United Arab Emirates (UAE) and the World Health Organization (WHO) have delivered a cargo plane carrying essential medical supplies to Sudan. The flight, which arrived at Khartoum International Airport this morning, carried vaccines, medical equipment, and essential medicines. The shipment, valued at $444,000, is the first major delivery of medical aid to Sudan since the outbreak of the conflict. The WHO had been stockpiling medical supplies before the conflict, but the delivery was delayed due to security concerns. The UAE's decision to send the aid comes as part of its ongoing efforts to support the Sudanese people and strengthen its relationship with the country, particularly in light of the current crisis. It also reflects the UAE's commitment to providing humanitarian assistance to countries in need, as well as its close ties with other countries in the region. Minister of International Cooperation Reem bint Ibrahim Al Hashimy said that the UAE is committed to working with the WHO to ensure the successful delivery of essential medical supplies and other aid to Sudan.

translations.source: Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa ta mayar da hankali ne musamman wajen samar wa kungiyoyin da suka fi fama da matsalar halin da ake ciki a Sudan, musamman ma marasa lafiya, da yara, da tsoffi, da mata wadanda suka fi fuskantar hadarin daga rikice-rikicen da ke ci gaba da faruwa. mafi matsananciyar gibi a cikin samar da magunguna da abinci. Ƙididdigar jin ƙai na Hadaddiyar Daular Larabawa tana nufin cewa ta tabbatar da cewa ta ci gaba da isar da saƙo ga duniya ƙaƙƙarfan sadaukarwarta ga ƙarfafa zaman lafiya, tsaro da kwanciyar hankali, ba kawai a wani yanki ba, har ma a duk faɗin duniya, tare da abokan hulɗarta da al'ummomin duniya. kasar na ci gaba da taimakawa al'ummar Sudan a lokutan rikici. Kayayyakin kiwon lafiyar sun haɗa da isassun raunuka, kayan aikin tiyata na gaggawa, da magunguna masu mahimmanci don kai ga mutane 165,000 da ke cikin matsananciyar buƙatar agajin jin kai. Cibiyoyin kiwon lafiya a faɗin ƙasar sun ba da rahoton cewa, kayayyakin kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum sun ƙare kuma basa samun damar shiga shaguna saida magunguna a ƙasar. Daga cikin tallafin hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) ta tura ƙwararrun likitocin gaggawa guda biyu da ke tafiya tare da tabbatar da cewa an rarraba waɗannan kayayyaki nan take zuwa manyan cibiyoyin kiwon lafiya goma sha uku don tallafawa wa ma’aikatan kiwon lafiya da kuma bada kulawa ga masu buƙatar agaji. Hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) tana da tan talatin na kayan agaji na zazzaɓin cizon sauro da cututtuka marasa yaɗuwa kamar ciwon suga hawan jini da kuma yanayin da ka iya haifar da asarar rai matuƙar ba’a ɗauki mataki ba. An gama shirya waɗannan haɗe da wajen ledar jini 23,000 a kamfanin jigila na duniya na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya a International Humanitarian City, sannan Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya tana ta neman hanyoyin da za ta kai waɗannan kaya zuwa Sudan da gaggawa, tare da haɗin guiwar Ma'aikatar Lafiya ta Sudan. Babban daraktan shirin agajin gaggawa ta hukumar lafiya ta duniya Dakta Micheal Ryan yace shirin agajin gaggawa na kamafani na jigila na duniya na hukumar lafiya ta duniya da haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa ta zama cibiyar samar da taimakon gaggawa a faɗin duniya. Taimakawa wa ƙasashe sama da ɗari da arba’in na a yankuna shida na hukumar lafiya ta duniya kamfanin na samar da hanyar rayuwa ga mambobin jihohin da ke fuskantar matsalolin kiwon lafiya da suka taso sanadiyyar ɓarkewar cututtuka bala’o’i kamar ambaliyar ruwa girgizar ƙasa da kuma tasirin rikicin akan tsarin kiwon lafiya. Tallafin da haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa ke bayarwa ya baiwa hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) kasancewa a sahun gaba a matakin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya da kuma isar da ƙwararrun likitoci ga masu buƙata a ko’ina a kuma duk lokacin da ake buƙata.
translations.reference: The UAE is particularly focused on providing for the most vulnerable groups affected by the situation in Sudan, especially the sick, children, the elderly, and women who are most at risk from the ongoing concerning conflict – and these relief flights will directly address the most pressing gaps in medical and food provision. The UAE's deeply held humanitarian values have meant that it has ensured it has continued to communicate to the world its robust and unrelenting dedication to strengthening peace, security and stability, not just regionally, but also worldwide, whilst alongside its partners and the international community, the country continues to assist the Sudanese people in times of crisis.” The health supplies include enough trauma, emergency surgical supplies, and essential medicines to immediately reach 165 000 people who are in desperate need of humanitarian aid. Health facilities across the country report they have run out of basic health supplies and that national medical stores are no longer accessible due to the security situation. Among other support, WHO is deploying two emergency logisticians travelling with the cargo to ensure that these supplies are immediately distributed to 13 major health facilities to support healthcare workers and extend care to those in need. WHO has another 30 metric tons of supplies for malaria and noncommunicable diseases like diabetes and hypertension, conditions that can become deadly if left untreated. These and about 23 000 blood bags are being readied within the WHO global logistics hub in the International Humanitarian City, and WHO is currently exploring all possibilities for delivering these supplies to Sudan as quickly as possible in collaboration with the Sudanese Ministry of Health. Dr Michael Ryan, Executive Director, of the WHO Health Emergencies Programme, said, “The WHO Global Logistics Hub in the UAE has become a centerpiece of rapid supply response to acute events globally. Supporting over 140 countries across all 6 WHO geographic regions, the hub provides a lifeline to Member States experiencing health emergencies arising from outbreaks of diseases, disasters such as floods and earthquakes, and the impact of conflict on health systems. The support provided by the UAE enables WHO to be at the forefront of these health emergency responses – delivering specialist medical aid to those in need wherever and whenever it is needed.
translations.hypothesis: The Arab League has focused its efforts on providing support to organizations working to assist vulnerable populations in Sudan, particularly those affected by conflict, including refugees, internally displaced persons, and women. The Arab League's humanitarian aid program aims to ensure that it continues to provide vital assistance to promote peace, security, and stability, not only within Sudan but also throughout the region and beyond. The League has also been working to support the Sudanese people during times of conflict. Its health assistance includes the provision of essential medicines, medical supplies, and equipment to over 165,000 people in need. Health facilities across the country have reported shortages of essential medicines and supplies. In response to the crisis, the World Health Organization (WHO) has dispatched two teams of medical experts to Sudan to help provide assistance. The teams are working to ensure that essential medicines and supplies are delivered to three major hospitals. The WHO is also working with the Sudanese Ministry of Health to transport additional supplies to the country.

translations.source: A ƙoƙarinta na gana ƙaƙƙarfan tsarin agajin gaggawa a faɗin duniya kulawa da kuma jajircewa haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa (UAE) da kuma hukumar lafiya ta duniya(WHO) da abokan haɗin gwiwar suna aiki don isar da kayayyakin don isa ga mutane masu rauni a duniya a lokacin da suke kan buƙata A nasa ɓangaren Daktan hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) na yankin gabashin bahar rum Dakta Ahmed Al-Mandhari yace hukumar lafiya na cigaba da haɗa kai da hukumomin kiwon lafiya na Sudan domin isar da muhimman kayayyakin kiwon lafiya daga dukkan hanyoyin da ake da su. Sakamakon rikicin asibitoci kantinan magani da cibiyoyin kula da lafiya a matakin farko sun tanadi muhimman maguguna don ceton rai. Zuwan ƙarin tan talatin na kayyakin agaji da tiyatar gaggawa da kuma muhimman magunguna zasu ba hukumar lafiya ta duniya damar tallafawa hukumomin lafiya goma sha uku da sake kafa ayyukan kula da lafiyar masu buƙatar hakan. Kamfanin mu na jigila na duniya a Dubai yana taka muhimmiyar rawa wurin tabbatar kayayyakin kiwon lafiya ba tare da katsewa ba kuma muna godiya da tallafin da haɗaɗdiyar daular larabawa ta bamu don sauƙaƙe mana na farko. Irin wannan taimakon kamar wannan shi ne yake nuna ƙoƙari a aikacenmu na yanki na samar da lafiya ba kowa. Tallafin lafiya da hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) ta bayar ta hannun haɗaɗɗiyar fdaular larabawa ya kai kusan kashi hamsin da biyar na jimilla kayayyakin jinya da aka baiwa Sudan daga ƙetare a shekarar 2022 wanda ke nuni da matsayin haɗaɗɗiyar daular larabawa a matsayin babbar cibiyar taimakon jinƙai ta ƙasa da ƙasa.
translations.reference: As part of efforts to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience the UAE, WHO, and partners are working hand-in-hand to deliver supplies to reach the world’s most vulnerable in their time of greatest need.” For his part, Dr Ahmed Al-Mandhari, WHO Regional Director for the Eastern Mediterranean, said, “The World Health Organization continues to coordinate with the Sudanese health authorities to deliver essential health supplies through all available pathways. Due to the conflict, hospitals, pharmacies and primary health care facilities have stocked out of essential, life-saving medicines. The arrival of an additional 30 metric tons of trauma and emergency surgery supplies as well as essential medicines will enable WHO to support 13 major health facilities and re-establish health care services for those in need. Our global logistics hub in Dubai plays a vital role in ensuring an uninterrupted supply of health commodities and we are grateful for the support provided by the United Arab Emirates to facilitate our first charter flight of these desperately needed supplies. It is support such as this that shows in action our Regional vision of health for all, by all.” The medical aid provided by the World Health Organization through the UAE accounted for approximately 55% of the total medical supplies provided to Sudan from abroad in 2022, reflecting the UAE's position as a major hub for international humanitarian assistance.
translations.hypothesis: The global effort to deliver essential medical supplies to vulnerable populations around the world is being coordinated by the World Health Organization (WHO), the United Arab Emirates (UAE), and a network of partners. Dr. Ahmed Al-Mandhari, a senior official at the WHO, stated that the organization would continue to work closely with the health authorities in Sudan to ensure that essential medicines and medical supplies reach those in need. The recent delivery of over 30 tons of medical supplies and equipment, including essential medicines, will enable the WHO to support three health facilities in Sudan and provide much-needed care to patients. Our global logistics company in Dubai is playing a vital role in ensuring that medical supplies are delivered safely and efficiently, and we are grateful for the support of the UAE, which has helped us to overcome significant logistical challenges. This kind of assistance is essential to our work in ensuring that everyone has access to the medical care they need. The financial support provided by the WHO, through the UAE, has enabled us to provide over 50% of the essential medicines that were distributed in Sudan in 2022, demonstrating the UAE's commitment to being a leading provider of humanitarian aid.

translations.source: Haɗin kai a cikin gida shine mabuɗin don ci gaba a duniya game da kiwon lafiya na muradan ƙarni SDGs Hukumomin 13 da suka sanya hannu kan Tsarin Ayyukan Duniya don Rayuwar Lafiya da Jin Dadin Kowa (SDG3 GAP) ne ke fitar da wani sabon rahoton ci gaba tare a haɗin gwiwa a yau. Rahoton mai taken me ya faru? mene bai yi ba? menen na gaba yana nuna shekaru huɗu na ayyukan haɗin gwiwa da manyan hukumomin ƙungiyoyi suka yi tare da bayyana abinda yayi aiki da wanda bai yi aiki ba domin ƙarfafa haɗin gwiwa da tallafi domin cigaban ƙasa a ɓangaren harkokin lafiya da kuma cigaban muradan ƙarni. A wannan shekarar ce aka kai rabin cimma cigaban muradan ƙarni amma kuma duniya tana baya wurin cimma muradan ƙarnin. Tun lokacin da aka ƙaddamar da shi a cikin 2019, SDG3 GAP ya ba da damar ƙirƙirar sabbin tsarin haɗin gwiwa tsakanin hukumomin da suka sanya hannu a cikin mahimman fannoni kamar dorewar kuɗi da kula da lafiya na farko da sauransu. Mafi ƙaranci ƙasashe 67 ne suka shiga ɗaya ko fiye da ɗaya daga cikin shirye-shiryen ingantata fasali na SDG3 GAP. Mun samu cigaba mai muhimmanci amma akwai amma har yanzu akwai doguwar tafiya domin inganta yadda ƙungiyoyi daban daban ke aiki tare domin tallafa wa ƙasashe. Dole ne mu saurari abinda ƙasashe ke gaya mana kuma muyi aiki bisa jagorancin su inji Dakta Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus darakta janar na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO kuma shugaban ƙungiyar shugabannin SDG3 GAP. Ina godiya ga abokan hulɗa bisa hadin kaida suka yi da kuma yadda suka nuna gaskiya cikin wannan rahoto. Rahoton ya bada shawarwari guda shida masu muhimmanci don tabbaatar da cewa ƙasashe sun ci gajiyar tallafin da ya dace da kuma tabbatar da alƙawuran da aka yi a SDG3 GAP.
translations.reference: Collaborating locally is key for progress globally towards health-related SDGs A new progress report is being jointly released today by the 13 signatory agencies of the Global Action Plan for Healthy Lives and Well-being for All (SDG3 GAP). The report entitled “What worked? What didn’t? What’s next?” reflects four years of joint work by major multilateral agencies outlining what has and has not worked in strengthening collaboration and support to accelerate country progress towards health-related Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). This year marks the halfway point toward the SDGs, but the world is lagging behind to reach the global goals. Since its launch in 2019, the SDG3 GAP enabled creation of new collaboration structures between the signatory agencies in key areas such as sustainable financing and primary health care among others. At least 67 countries engaged in on one or more of the SDG3 GAP accelerator themes. "We have made important progress, but we still have a long path to travel to improve the way that multilateral organizations work together to support countries. We must listen to what countries tell us and act upon their guidance,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General and Chair of the SDG3 GAP Principals Group. “I thank the partners for their collaboration and for the honest self-assessments contained in this report. "The report provides six key recommendations to ensure that countries benefit from a more streamlined support and to realize the commitments made in the SDG3 GAP.
translations.hypothesis: Collaboration within the home is essential for achieving global progress in the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs). The SDG3 GAP, a new report released today by a coalition of major organizations, highlights the progress and challenges of collaborative efforts to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals for health and well-being for all (SDG3). The report, titled "What has worked? What hasn't?," presents data from four years of collaborative work, showing what has been successful and what has not in promoting health and well-being in developing countries. While significant progress has been made towards achieving the SDGs, the world is still far from reaching its goals. Since the launch of the SDG3 GAP in 2019, the initiative has provided a platform for collaboration between organizations on key issues such as financing and primary health care. More than 67 countries have participated in one or more of the SDG3 GAP initiatives. We have seen significant progress, but there is still a long way to go to ensure that all countries have the resources they need to achieve the SDGs.
