We must listen to what countries are telling us and work together to support their efforts, as emphasized by WHO Director-General Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and the SDG3 GAP Steering Committee. I would like to thank our partners for their collaboration and for their commitment to transparency in this report. The report provides six key recommendations for ensuring that countries receive the appropriate support and that the goals of the SDG3 GAP are met.

translations.source: Sun haɗa da: Cigaba da inganta cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG3 GAP na lafiya ta hanyar neman ra’ayoyin mambobin ƙasashe kan yadda muke haɗa kai a matakin ƙasa da kuma amsa shawarwarin da suka danganci; Kula da GAP a matsayin dandamali da haɗin gwiwa; Haɓaka haɗin gwiwa mai ƙarfi a matakin ƙasa kan kula da hukumar kiwon lafiya a matakin farko da kuma bincika sabbin batutuwa kamar tsarin kiwon lafiya mai jure yanayi Tare da yin amfani da sabbin hanyoyin a matakin ƙasa domin isar da tsari mai tasiri; Asusun Yara na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNICEF); Unitaid; Aiki tare da mambobin ƙasashe don ƙarfafa gwiwa ta hanyar jagoranci a siyasance da kuma jagoranci na shugabanci da kudi domin tallafawa da haɗin gwiwa. Mihimman binciken da aka samu na rahoton da aka samu na rahoton zai bada labari game da tattaunawar babban taron majalisar dinkin duniya na cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG da kuma taron duniya na akan kiwon lafiya a watan Satumbar 2023. Watanni kafin waɗannan muhimman abubuwan da suka faru sun bada dama ga haɗin gwiwar ƙasashe membobi da hukumomi da ƙungiyoyi daban daban don gano hanyoyin da za’a aiwatar da shawarwarin da shiryawa don yin ƙarfaffan haɗin gwiwa tare da haɓaka cigaba ga cigaban muradan ƙarni masu alaƙa da kiwon lafiya na rabin lokacin da SDG ta dauka. Bayanan kula ga Edita:
translations.reference: These include: continue the SDG3 GAP improvement cycle for health by seeking the views of Member States on how we collaborate at country-level and responding to related recommendations; maintain GAP as a platform for collaboration; foster stronger collaboration at the country level on primary health care and explore new thematic topics such as climate resilient health systems; jointly apply new approaches at country level such as the delivery for impact approach; engage more with civil society; and work with Member States to strengthen incentives for collaboration through political leadership, governance direction and funding to support collaboration. Key findings of the report will feed into discussions of the next United Nations General Assembly SDG Summit and the High-level Meeting on Universal Health Coverage in September 2023. The months leading up to these key events provide an opportunity for a joint push by Member States and multilateral agencies to identify ways on how to implement the recommendations and to prepare the ground to fully leverage stronger collaboration and accelerate progress towards the health-related SDGs in the second half of the SDG timeframe. Note to the editor:
translations.hypothesis: These include: Continuing to improve progress towards the SDG3 goal of healthy lives and well-being for all by seeking input from country representatives on how to strengthen national health systems and implement relevant recommendations; Strengthening the partnership between the Global Fund and country-led initiatives; Enhancing strong national partnerships for monitoring and evaluating the Global Fund; The UNICEF; Unitaid; and working with country partners to strengthen their capacity through leadership and financial support. The key findings from the report will provide insights into the discussions at the High-Level Meeting of the Global Fund and the International Conference on Global Health in September 2023. In the months leading up to these important events, there has been a concerted effort to bring together country representatives, governments, and civil society organizations to identify and implement effective strategies for strengthening partnerships and advancing progress towards SDG3-related health goals.

translations.source: SDG GAP ya bayyana yadda hukumomi goma sha da suka sahannu zasu yi amfani da sabbin hanyoyin aiki, tare. Masu rattaba hannu ga ciga cigaban muradan ƙarni SDG3 GAP sune Gavi, da kuma ƙungiyar allurar rigakafi Kayan Tallafi na Duniya don Mata, Yara da Matasa (GFF); Asusun duniya don yaƙi da cutar AIDS,tarin fuka da zazzaɓin cizon sauro(Asusun duniya) Ƙungiyar ƙwadago ta duniya (ILO); Shirin haɗin gwiwa na majalisar ɗinkin duniya kan HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); Shirin cigaban majalisar ɗinkin duniya (UNDP) Asusun majalisar ɗinkin duniya na yawan jama’a (UNFPA); Asusun Yara na Majalisar Dinkin Duniya (UNICEF); Unitaid; Ƙungiyar majalisar ɗinkin duniya don daidaita jinsi da ƙarfafa mata (matan majalisar ɗinkin duniya)
translations.reference: The SDG3 GAP describes how the 13 signatory agencies will adopt new ways of working, building on existing successful collaborations, and jointly align their support around national plans and strategies that are country-owned and -led. The signatories to the SDG3 GAP are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance; Global Financing Facility for Women, Children and Adolescents (GFF); Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria (Global Fund); International Labour Organization (ILO); Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); United Nations Children’s Fund (UNICEF); Unitaid; United Nations Entity for Gender Equality and the Empowerment of Women (UN Women);
translations.hypothesis: The SDG Gap report outlines how ten partner organizations will work together to achieve the Sustainable Development Goals. The key partners in the SDG3 Gap initiative are Gavi, the Vaccine Alliance, and the Global Fund for Women, Children and Youth (GFF); the Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria; the International Labour Organization (ILO); the Joint United Nations Programme on HIV/AIDS (UNAIDS); the United Nations Development Programme (UNDP); the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA); UNICEF; Unitaid; and the United Nations Population Fund (UNFPA).

translations.source: Rukunin bankin duniya; Shirn abincin na duniya (WFP); da kuma Hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO). Duba cikakken rahoto me yayi aiki? Menene na gaba?
translations.reference: World Bank Group; World Food Programme (WFP); and World Health Organization (WHO). See full report, What Worked? What’s next?
translations.hypothesis: The World Bank; the World Food Programme (WFP); and the World Health Organization (WHO). See the full report for details? What are the next steps?

translations.source: Sabon sakamakon binciken ya nuna tsarin kiwon lafiya ya fara murmurewa da annobar Bayan shekaru uku na cutar COVID-19 tsarin kiwon lafiya a cikin ƙasashe sun fara nuna alamun farko na farfaɗowar tsarin kiwon lafiya a cewar rahoton wucin gadi na hukumar lafiya ta duniya (WHO) game da zagaye na huɗu na binciken bugun jini na duniya kan cigaba da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya a lokacin annobar COVID-19 Nuwaba 2022 January 2023. Ya zuwa farkon shekarar 2023, kasashe sun ba da rahoton samun raguwar cikas wajen isar da ayyukan kiwon lafiya na yau da kullun, amma sun nuna bukatar saka hannun jari don farfadowa da kuma karfin juriya na gaba. A cikin kasashe 139 da suka amsa zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO kasashe sun ba da rahoton ci gaba da wargajewa a kusan kashi daya bisa hudu na ayyuka a matsakaici. A cikin ƙasashe 84 inda za a iya nazarin yanayin, yawan ayyukan da aka katse ya ragu a matsaikata daga 56% a watan Yuli-Satumba 2020 zuwa 23% a watan Nuwamba 2022- Janairu 2023. Ci gaba da rikice-rikicen ya faru ne saboda abubuwan buƙatu- da abubuwan wadata, gami da ƙarancin matakan neman kiwon lafiya a cikin al'ummomi gami da ƙarancin wadatar ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da sauran albarkatun kula da lafiya kamar buɗaɗɗen asibitoci ko samfuran magunguna da samfuran da ake samu. Abin maraba ne cewa tsarin kiwon lafiya a yawancin kasashe sun fara dawo da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya ga miliyoyin mutanen da suka rasa su yayin barkewar cutar," in ji Dr Rudi Eggers, Daraktan Hukumar Kula da Lafiya ta Duniya. Amma muna buƙatar tabbatar da cewa dukkan ƙasashe suna ci gaba da rufe wannan rata don dawo da ayyukan kiwon lafiya, da kuma amfani da darussan da aka koya don gina shirye-shiryen kiwon lafiya da tsayayye don nan gaba". Alamun farko na samun lafiya A cikin wannan sabon binciken, ƙasashe kaɗan sun ba da rahoton ƙaddamar da damar samun dama ga duk dandamali na isar da sabis da mahimman ayyukan kiwon lafiyar jama'a tun daga rahoton 2020-2021, suna nuna muhimmin mataki na komawa zuw matakin kafin annoba da isar da sabis na da faffadan tsarin aiki.
translations.reference: New survey results show health systems starting to recover from pandemic After three years of the COVID-19 pandemic, health systems in countries have started showing the first major signs of health system recovery, according to the WHO interim report on the “Fourth round of the global pulse survey on continuity of essential health services during the COVID-19 pandemic: November 2022–January 2023”. By early 2023, countries reported experiencing reduced disruptions in the delivery of routine health services, but highlighted the need to invest in recovery and stronger resilience for the future. Among the 139 countries that responded to the fourth round of WHO’s pulse survey, countries reported continued disruptions in almost one-quarter of services on average. In 84 countries where trend analysis is possible, the percentage of disrupted services declined on average from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022- January 2023. Persisting disruptions are due to both demand- and supply-side factors, including low levels of health care-seeking in communities as well as limited availability of health workers and other health-care resources such as open clinics or available stocks of medicines and products. “It is welcome news that health systems in the majority of countries are starting to restore essential health services for millions of people who missed them during the pandemic,” said Dr Rudi Eggers, WHO Director for Integrated Health Services. “But we need to ensure that all countries continue to close this gap to recover health services, and apply lessons learnt to build more prepared and resilient health systems for the future”. First notable signs of recovery In this new survey, fewer countries reported intentionally scaling back access across all service delivery platforms and essential public health functions since 2020-2021 reporting, showing an important step to return to pre-pandemic levels of service delivery and broader system functioning.
translations.hypothesis: The new report indicates that the healthcare system has begun to recover from the impact of the COVID-19 pandemic. Over the past three years, healthcare systems in countries around the world have experienced a decline in the availability of essential health services, according to a report by the World Health Organization (WHO) on the fourth round of the Global Health Security Agenda (GHSA) survey on the ongoing importance of health security during the COVID-19 pandemic. By early 2023, countries have reported a decline in the number of essential health services provided, but they have also indicated a need for increased investment and preparedness. In the 139 countries that participated in the fourth round of the GHSA survey, countries reported a decline in the availability of essential health services in approximately 40% of cases. In the 84 countries where data was available, the number of essential health services that were disrupted declined from 56% in July-September 2020 to 23% in November 2022-January 2023. The decline in essential health services was due to a number of factors, including a shortage of healthcare workers, a lack of resources, and a decline in the availability of essential medicines and supplies. "The healthcare system in many countries has begun to restore essential health services to the millions of people who were affected by the pandemic," said Dr. Rudi Eggers, Director of the World Health Organization. "However, we need to ensure that all countries continue to make progress in this area, and that we use the lessons learned to build more resilient health systems for the future."

translations.source: A ƙarshen 2022, yawancin ƙasashe sun ba da rahoton alamun dawo da sabis na ɓangare, gami da sabis don jima'i, haihuwa, uwa, jarirai, yara da matasa; abinci mai gina jiki; rigakafi; cututtukan cututtuka (gami da zazzabin cizon sauro, HIV, tarin fuka, da sauran cututtukan da ake ɗauka ta hanyar jima'i); cuta mai zafi da aka manta da ita; cututtukan da ba a kamuwa da su; kula da rashin lafiyar taɓin ƙwaƙwalwa, jijiyoyin jiki da kuma amfani da kwayoyi; kula da tsofaffi; da kuma na gargajiya da / ko ƙarin kulawa. Adadin kasashen da ke bayar da rahoton cikas ga tsarin samar da kayayyaki na kasa ya ragu daga kusan rabin (29 daga cikin kasashe 59 da suka amsa) zuwa kusan kashi hudu (18 daga cikin kasashe 66 da suka amsa) a cikin shekarar da ta gabata. Duk da alamun farfadowa, hargitsin sabis na ci gaba da wanzuwa a cikin ƙasashe a duk yankuna da matakan samun kudin shiga, kuma a cikin mafi yawan saitunan isar da sabis da wuraren sabis na ganowa. Ƙasashe kuma suna fuskantar karuwar bayanan sabis - mafi yawan lokuta a cikin sabis don tantancewa, ganowa da kuma magance cututtukan da ba sa yaduwa - wanda zai haifar da mummunan sakamako yayin da mutane ke jinkirta samun kulawa akan lokaci. Farfado da mahimman isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya yana da mahimmanci saboda rikice-rikice - gami da sabis don haɓaka kiwon lafiya, rigakafin cututtuka, gwaje gwajd, jiyya, gyare-gyare - na iya haifar da mummunar illa ga lafiyar jama'a da kuma mutum fiye da annobar kanta, musamman ma. tsakanin jama'a masu rauni. Haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 a cikin mahimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya A wani muhimmin mataki na dawo da tsarin da sauyawa, yawancin ƙasashe sun sami ci gaba wajen haɗa ayyukan COVID-19 a cikin isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum. Kimanin kashi 80-90% na ƙasashe sun haɗa allurar rigakafin COVID-19, bincike da sabis na kula da lamura da kuma sabis don yanayin bayan COVID-19 cikin isar da sabis na yau da kullum. Har yanzu, yawancin ƙasashe (80% na 83 na ƙasashe masu ba da amsa) sun ba da rahoton aƙalla ƙugiya ɗaya don haɓaka damar yin amfani da mahimman kayan aikin COVID-19 (misali COVID-19 bincike, warkewa, alluran rigakafi da kayan kariya na sirri - PPE), tare da batutuwan ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya da rashin kuɗi da ke wakiltar mafi yawan shinge. Ƙarin tallafi da ake buƙata don farfadowa, juriya da shirye-shirye Yawancin ƙasashe sun fara amfani da abin da suka koya yayin annobar COVID-19, gami da aiwatar da wasu sabbin dabarun rage rikicewar sabis a cikin isar da sabis na kiwon lafiya na yau da kullum.
translations.reference: By the end of 2022, most countries reported partial signs of service recovery, including in services for sexual, reproductive, maternal, newborn, child and adolescent health; nutrition; immunization; communicable diseases (including malaria, HIV, TB, and other sexually-transmitted infections); neglected tropical diseases; noncommunicable diseases; management of mental, neurological and substance use disorders; care for older people; and traditional and/or complementary care. The number of countries reporting disruption to their national supply chain system reduced from nearly half (29 of 59 responding countries) to about a quarter (18 of 66 responding countries) within the last year. Despite signs of recovery, service disruptions persist across countries in all regions and income levels, and across most service delivery settings and tracer service areas. Countries are also dealing with increasing service backlogs – most frequently in services for screening, diagnosis and treatment of noncommunicable diseases – which can lead to negative consequences as people are delayed access to timely care. Recovering essential health service delivery is critical because disruptions – including to services for health promotion, disease prevention, diagnosis, treatment, rehabilitation and palliation – may have even greater adverse health effects at population and individual level than the pandemic itself, especially among vulnerable populations. Integrating COVID-19 services into essential health services In another important step towards system recovery and transition, most countries have made progress in integrating COVID-19 services into routine health service delivery. About 80-90% of countries have fully integrated COVID-19 vaccination, diagnostic and case management services as well as services for post COVID-19 condition into routine service delivery. Still, most countries (80% of 83 responding countries) reported at least one bottleneck to scaling up access to essential COVID-19 tools (e.g. COVID-19 diagnostics, therapeutics, vaccines and personal protective equipment - PPE), with health workforce issues and lack of funding representing the most common barriers. Further support needed for recovery, resilience and preparedness Most countries have started to apply what they have learnt during the COVID-19 pandemic, including through the institutionalization of a number of innovative service disruption mitigation strategies into routine health service delivery.
translations.hypothesis: By the end of 2022, most countries had reported significant disruptions to essential health services, including services for maternal and newborn care, immunization, treatment ofcommunicable diseases, non-communicable diseases (including respiratory infections, HIV, tuberculosis, and other vaccine-preventable diseases), surgery and emergency care; and mental health services. The number of countries reporting disruptions to essential health services decreased from about one-third (29 of 59 countries surveyed) to about one-quarter (18 of 66 countries surveyed) in the previous year. Despite these disruptions, essential health services continued to be available in most countries, although access was often limited by factors such as the availability of health facilities and healthcare providers, and by the affordability of care. Many countries also faced challenges in scaling up their essential health services, particularly in providing services for non-communicable diseases, which require more specialized and intensive care. The COVID-19 pandemic has further exacerbated these challenges, as many countries have had to divert resources from essential health services to respond to the pandemic. A key priority for countries is to restore and strengthen their essential health services, in order to protect the health and well-being of their populations. This includes ensuring that all people have access to essential health services, regardless of their income, location, or other factors.

translations.source: Waɗannan sun haɗa da ƙaddamar da hanyoyin sadarwar telemedicine, haɓaka kulawar gida ko ayyukan kulawa da kai, hanyoyin ƙarfafa wadatar ma'aikatan kiwon lafiya, iyawa da hanyoyin tallafi, sabbin abubuwa a cikin samarwa da isar da magunguna da kayayyaki, ƙarin hanyoyin sadarwa na yau da kullun, da haɗin gwiwa tare da kamfanoni masu zaman kansu. masu bayarwa. Kashi uku cikin huɗu na ƙasashe sun ba da rahoton ƙarin tallafin kuɗi don dawo da tsarin dogon lokaci, juriya da shirye shirye. sanarwa ga editoci: A zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na na hukumar lafiya ta duniyaWHO na duniya, an gayyaci kasashe, yankuna da yankuna 222 don amsa daidaitaccen binciken yanar gizo tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 da Janairu 2023. Sanarwa ga editoci: A zagaye na hudu na binciken bugun jini na na hukumar lafiya ta duniyaWHO na duniya, an gayyaci kasashe, yankuna da yankuna 222 don amsa daidaitaccen binciken yanar gizo tsakanin Nuwamba 2022 da Janairu 2023. Binciken na hudu ya biyo bayan binciken hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO na baya na 2020 da 2021: Zagaye na 1 (Mayu-Satumba 2020), Zagaye na 2 (Janairu-Maris 2021), da Zagaye na 3 (Nuwamba-Disamba 2021) wanda ya nuna girman annobar ta shafi ci gaba da muhimman ayyukan kiwon lafiya da kuma yadda kasashe ke daukar matakai. Yayin da binciken bugun jini yake da iyakoki kamar bayar da rahoto na ɓangaranci da wakilci, ƙarfin wannan ƙoƙarin shine yana da cikakke kuma yana ba da bayanai cikin sauri. Yakamata a fahimci kalmar "ƙasa" ta ƙunshi dukkan kasashe, yankuna da wurare. An kammala nazarin abubuwan da ke faruwa bisa la'akari da martani daga ƙasashe 84, yankuna, ko wurare da suka amsa aƙalla sashin bincike ɗaya a cikin duk zagayen binciken bugun jini guda huɗu.
translations.reference: These include deployment of telemedicine approaches, promotion of home-based care or self-care interventions, approaches for strengthening health workers availability, capacities and support mechanisms, innovations in procuring and delivering medicines and supplies, more routine community communications, and partnerships with private sector providers. Three quarters of countries reported additional funding allocation towards longer term system recovery, resilience and preparedness. Countries expressed need for WHO support to address remaining challenges in the COVID-19 context and beyond, most frequently related to health workforce strengthening, building the monitoring capacities of health services, designing primary health care -- oriented models of care, governance, policy and planning and financial planning and funding. Note to editors: In the fourth round of WHO’s global pulse survey, 222 countries, territories and areas were invited to respond to a standardized web-based survey between November 2022 and January 2023. The fourth survey followed up on WHO’s previous 2020 and 2021 pulse surveys: Round 1 (May-September 2020), Round 2 (January-March 2021), and Round 3 (November-December 2021) which showed the extent to which the pandemic was affecting the continuity of essential health services and how countries are taking action. While pulse surveys have limitations such as reporting bias and representativeness, the strength of this effort is that it is comprehensive and delivers information rapidly. The term “country” should be understood to include all countries, territories and areas. Trend analyses was completed based on responses from 84 countries, territories, or areas that responded to at least one survey section in all four pulse survey rounds.
translations.hypothesis: These include the implementation of telemedicine communication methods, the development of home care or self-care programs, strategies to support healthcare workers, financial assistance and support mechanisms, new innovations in the production and distribution of medicines and supplies, enhanced communication channels, and collaboration with private sector partners. Approximately 84 countries, regions, and areas have reported increased funding for long-term recovery, resilience, and preparedness efforts. Note to editors: In the fourth phase of the World Health Organization's (WHO) global survey, 84 countries, regions, and areas were invited to participate in a standardized online survey between November 2022 and January 2023. Note to editors: In the fourth phase of the World Health Organization's (WHO) global survey, 84 countries, regions, and areas were invited to participate in a standardized online survey between November 2022 and January 2023. This survey followed the previous WHO surveys of 2020 and 2021: Phase 1 (May-September 2020), Phase 2 (January-March 2021), and Phase 3 (November-December 2021), which highlighted the ongoing and significant impact of the pandemic on healthcare systems and the measures taken by countries. While the survey has limitations such as the need for detailed reporting and representation, the overall effort is comprehensive and provides timely data. It is important to note that the term "country" encompasses all countries, regions, and areas. The analysis is based on responses from 84 countries, regions, or areas that participated in at least one phase of the four-phase global survey.

translations.source: Sanarwa na bikin cika shekaru 50 na HRP: Sweden da daular Ingila ta Birtaniya arewacin Ireland Don ɗaukar siyasa mai rai membobin Kwamitin Manufofi da Gudanarwa (PCC) waɗanda ke bikin cika shekaru 50 na HRP da nasarorin da aka samu, Sweden da daular Ingila ta Birtaniya da arewacin Ireland karanta wannan bayanin godiya da taya murna a taron na 36th na HRP. Farashin PCC. Zamaninmu ya sami ci gaba a fannin kimiyya da fasaha fiye da sauran al'ummomin da suka gabata tare kuma sun shaidi sauye-sauye da dama na ilimi, fasaha, likitanci da zamantakewa wanda juyin juya halin hana daukar ciki ya jagoranta sannan kuma ya biyo bayan ci gaban kiwon lafiyar haihuwa, sabbin hanyoyin kiwon lafiya gami da hakkoki da daidaiton jinsi. Kodayake, manyan ƙalubale a fagen SRHR suna ci gaba, kuma, a zahiri, ana sake tambayar wasu ci gaba a yau. Muna yaba wa ma'aikatan HRP-, abokan tarayya da masu goyan baya kan nasarorin da aka samu har zuwa yau kuma mun ga babban ci gaba da aka cimma a cikin rabin karni na samar da shaidun da ke goyan bayan cikakkiyar dama ga lafiyar jima'i da haihuwa da hakki. Muna so mu fayyace manyan nau'ikan nasarori guda uku: Da fari dai, babban bincike na HRP ya yi tasiri mai ma'ana kan manufofin kiwon lafiyar jama'a, ka'idoji, da ayyuka a duk duniya don magance buƙatun ci gaba na al'umma daban-daban. Na biyu, HRP, musamman ta hanyar HRP Alliance, ya ƙarfafa cibiyoyin bincike a cikin gida da kuma na duniya, kuma, ya ba wa masu bincike da yawa damar samun ƙwarewar bincike wanda ke tabbatar da cewa bincike mai inganci na SRHR zai iya ci gaba, kuma ya haɓaka, a nan gaba. Na uku, a cikin duniyar da ke fama da tashin hankali, rikice-rikice da ƙaruwar adawa ga daidaiton jinsi, HRP ta sauƙaƙe kiyaye haƙƙin mata da 'yan mata da kuma al'ummomi masu rauni. HRP ya kasance babban kayan aiki a cikin tsarin Majalisar Dinkin Duniya don bincike game da haifuwar ɗan adam kuma ci gaba da kasancewa jagora musamman a lafiyar jima'i da haihuwa ta duniya.
translations.reference: Statement of celebration for HRP’s 50th anniversary: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland To capture the spirit of Policy and Coordination Committee (PCC) members celebrating HRP’s 50th anniversary and its numerous achievements, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read the following note of appreciation and congratulations at the 36th meeting of the HRP PCC. Our generation has seen more progress in science and technology than all preceding generations together and witnessed several intellectual, technological, medical and social transformation spearheaded by the contraceptive revolution and followed by advances in reproductive health, health innovations as well as rights and gender equality. However, considerable challenges in the field of SRHR continue, and, arguably, some advances are in question again today. We applaud HRP-staff, partners and supporters on the numerous achievements to date and recognise the enormous strides accomplished during half a century in producing the evidence that supports full and equal access to sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to outline three major categories of achievements: Firstly, the high-quality research by HRP has had tangible impact on public health policy, standards, and practice worldwide addressing the evolving needs of diverse populations. Secondly, HRP, notably through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research institutions locally and globally, and, enabled numerous researchers to acquire the research skills that ensure that high-quality SRHR research can continue, and indeed increase, into the future. Thirdly, in a world beset by crisis, conflict and increasing resistance to gender equality, HRP has facilitated the upholding of the basic rights of women and girls and vulnerable populations. HRP remains the main instrument within the United Nations system for research in human reproduction and continues to be an exceptional leader in global sexual and reproductive health.
translations.hypothesis: Announcement of the 50th Anniversary of HRP: Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland Celebrate the achievements of the Committee on Population and Development (PCC) in marking the 50th anniversary of HRP, Sweden and the United Kingdom of Great Britain and Northern Ireland read a statement of gratitude and congratulations at the 36th HRP conference. The PCC has made significant progress in the field of population, health, and development, and has witnessed numerous changes in education, technology, medicine, and social welfare. These changes have been driven by a commitment to human rights and equality, and have led to improvements in reproductive health, new health technologies, and gender equality. Despite these advances, significant challenges remain in the field of SRHR, and some progress has been reversed in recent years. We commend the staff of HRP, its partners, and its supporters for their achievements to date, and we are pleased to see the significant progress that has been made in the past half-century in promoting the full enjoyment of sexual and reproductive health and rights. We would like to highlight three key achievements: First, the HRP has had a significant impact on the field of population, health, and development, and has helped to shape policies and practices around the world. Second, HRP, particularly through the HRP Alliance, has strengthened research capacity both domestically and internationally, and has provided researchers with the skills and resources they need to conduct high-quality SRHR research. Third, in a world of conflict, instability, and rising opposition to gender equality, HRP has played a vital role in protecting the rights of women and girls and promoting the well-being of vulnerable populations. HRP has been a key partner in the UN system for research on human reproduction and continues to be a leading voice in the field of sexual and reproductive health and rights.

translations.source: HRP da takwarorinta sun kusantar da mu ga alkawarin da aka yi na taron kasa da kasa kan yawan jama’a da ci gaba a shekarar 1994 da kuma kiran da ta yi na sanya hakkin daidaikun mutane, musamman mata da ‘yan mata a kan tushen cigaban muradan ƙarni. Muna so mu bayyana godiya da jinjina ga sakatariyar HRP, abokan hulɗarta, masu ba da tallafi da cibiyoyin haɗin gwiwar duniya da masana da ke taimakawa wajen tabbatar da kokarinta na taimakawa wajen kawo canji ga mata, maza, yara, matasa da kuma al'ummomi daban-daban. HRP ta himmatu don yin hidima. A madadin membobin PCC, ta bamu damar sake tabbatar da haɗin gwiwa tare da ku don tallafawa nasarorin HRP, wanda nasarar 2030 Agenda ya dogara.
translations.reference: HRP and its partners have brought us closer to the promise of the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development and its call to put the rights of individuals, especially women and girls, at the heart of sustainable development. We want to express our appreciation and gratitude to the HRP secretariat, its partners, co-sponsors and the global network of collaborating centres and experts helping to ensure its efforts contribute to making a difference to the women, men, children, adolescents and various communities HRP is committed to serve. On behalf of PCC members, allow us to reaffirm our partnership with you to support the achievements of HRP, upon which the success of the 2030 Agenda depends.
translations.hypothesis: The HRP and its partners have been instrumental in upholding the commitments made at the 1994 International Conference on Population and Development, particularly the call for the empowerment of women and girls as a key to sustainable development. We would like to express our gratitude and appreciation to the HRP, its partners, donors, and global networks of experts who have worked tirelessly to support its efforts to promote gender equality and women's empowerment. The HRP is committed to making a difference. As representatives of the PCC, we are proud to be part of this effort and to work with you to achieve the goals of the 2030 Agenda.

translations.source: Hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta ƙaddamar da wani sabon shiri don inganta shirye-shiryen annoba Don taimaka wa ƙasashe su shirya don faruwar annoba a gaba,hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta ƙaddamar da wani sabon shiri a yau wanda ke ba da jagora kan haɗaɗɗun tsare-tsare don ba da amsa ga duk wani cututtukan numfashi kamar mura ko coronaviruses. Sabon Tsari da Juriya don Ƙaddamar da wani tsari, ko PRET, ta haɗa sabbin kayan aiki da hanyoyin ilmantarwa da ayyukan gama kai da aka kafa yayin bala'in COVID-19 da sauran abubuwan gaggawa na lafiyar jama'a na baya-bayan nan. Ta hanyar shirin hukumar lafiya ta duniya, WHO za ta yi amfani da tsarin watsa shirye-shirye don jagorantar kasashe a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba, ganin cewa da dama sun zama ruwan dare tsakanin kungiyoyin masu cutar. PRET tana amsa kiran jagorar fasaha da goyan baya don haɓaka da ƙarfafa shirye-shirye a haɗe da amsa, kamar yadda aka tsara a cikin kudurori na Majalisar Lafiya ta Duniya. Cutar ta COVID-19 da sauran matsalolin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya sun nuna cewa akwai bukatar kasashe su kasance cikin shiri don magance barazanar kamuwa da cuta, tare da tsare-tsaren shirye-shiryen da aka kera a hannu da ingantacciyar daidaituwa da hadin gwiwa da sauran bangarorin kamar aikin gona. Tsarin shirye-shirye, rigakafi, da mayar da martani ba dole ba ne su kasance lardin da ke bangaren lafiya kaɗai bai,” in ji Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Darakta Janar na hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO. Kamar yadda matsalolin gaggawa na kiwon lafiya ke yin tasiri a sassa da dama, haka nan dole ne a shirin mu da kokarin mayar da martani ga ɓangarori, darussa da cututtuka. Yana da mahimmanci kuma, cewa haɗin kai da daidaito tsakanin al'umma su ne cibiyar ƙoƙarinmu, musamman ga waɗanda ba a sani ba kuma galibi suna cikin haɗari. PRET juyin halitta ne a tsarin WHO game da shirye-shiryen kamuwa da cutar ta hanyar aikace-aikacen yanayin watsa ruwan tabarau, maimakon mayar da hankali kan takamaiman cututtuka.
translations.reference: WHO launches new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness To help countries better prepare for future pandemics, WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrated planning for responding to any respiratory pathogen such as influenza or coronaviruses. The new Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats Initiative, or PRET, incorporates the latest tools and approaches for shared learning and collective action established during the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent public health emergencies. Through the initiative, WHO will use a mode of transmission approach to guide countries in pandemic planning, given that many capacities and capabilities are common among groups of pathogens. PRET answers the call for technical guidance and support for promoting and strengthening integrated preparedness and response, as outlined in World Health Assembly resolutions. The COVID-19 pandemic and other health emergencies have shown that countries need to be operationally ready to respond to infectious disease threats, with tailored preparedness plans in hand and better coordination and collaboration with other sectors such as agriculture. “Preparedness, prevention, and response activities must not be the province of the health sector alone,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, WHO Director-General. “Just as health emergencies have impacts across many sectors, so must our preparedness and response efforts span sectors, disciplines and pathogens. It is critical, too, that community engagement and equity are the centre of our efforts, especially for those populations that are marginalized and most at risk.” PRET is an evolution in WHO’s approach to pandemic preparedness through the application of a mode of transmission lens, rather than a focus on specific diseases.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) has launched a new initiative to improve pandemic preparedness. In an effort to help countries prepare for future pandemics, the WHO launched a new initiative today that provides guidance on integrating various strategies to respond to respiratory diseases such as influenza and coronaviruses. The new initiative, called the Pandemic Preparedness and Response Framework (PPRF), includes new tools and approaches for surveillance, detection, and response to public health emergencies, including the COVID-19 pandemic and other recent outbreaks. Through the PPRF, the WHO will work with countries to develop and implement pandemic preparedness plans, ensuring that they are aligned with international standards and best practices. The COVID-19 pandemic and other recent health emergencies have highlighted the need for countries to be prepared to respond to the threat of infectious diseases, with plans in place to detect and contain outbreaks. "Pandemic preparedness is not just the responsibility of the health sector," said Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO. "It requires a coordinated effort involving all sectors of society, including government, the private sector, and civil society.

translations.source: WHO za ta ci gaba da haɓakawa da yada jagora kan takamaiman cututtuka kamar yadda ake buƙata. Kaddamar da shirin yana gudana ne a rana ta ƙarshe ta tarurruka tare da mutane sama da 120 da ke wakiltar ƙasashe membobin, abokan tarayya, da sakatariyar WHO, don ayyana hanyar ci gaba don PRET, gami da haɓaka taswirar aiwatarwa ta duniya don shirye-shiryen rigakafin cututtukan ƙwayar cuta na numfashi. Tsarin farko na PRET Initiative yana mai da hankali kan ƙwayoyin cuta na numfashi, gami da mura, coronaviruses, da ƙwayar cuta ta syncytial na numfashi. Ganin yadda annobar cutar ta COVID-19 take cigaba da kuma yuwuwar barazanar kamuwa da mura ta jiragen ruwa, wannan tsarin zai baiwa kasashe damar yin nazari sosai, gwadawa, da sabunta yunƙurin shirye-shiryen cutar sankarau don tabbatar da cewa suna da iya aiki da iya aiki a wurin. Ana ci gaba da aiki don gano rukuni na gaba na ƙwayoyin cuta, irin su arboviruses, da za a magance a ƙarƙashin wannan shirin. Wannan zai bi manyan abubuwan da aka gano ta hanyar shawarwari goma don ƙarfafa gine-ginen duniya don shirye-shiryen gaggawa na lafiya, amsawa, da juriya (HEPR). Ƙaddamar da PRET ta haifar da sabon zamani don shirye-shiryen cutar sankara kuma tana wakiltar juyin halitta na ainihin ayyukan hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO don tallafawa duk Membobin Kasashe don ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen gaggawa na kiwon lafiya, rigakafi, da damar amsawa da iyawa. Har ila yau, shirin na iya yin aiki don aiwatar da manufofi da tanade-tanade na Yarjejeniyar Cutar Kwalara, wadda a halin yanzu ƙasashe membobin hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ke tattaunawa. Bayan bayanai da tattaunawa tsakanin mahalarta taron, hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO ta gayyaci masu ruwa da tsaki zuwa wannan kiran na duniya don aiwatar da shirye-shiryen rigakafin cutar sankarau. Kira zuwa ga Aiki
translations.reference: WHO will continue to develop and disseminate guidance on specific diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative is taking place on the last day of meetings with over 120 individuals representing Member States, partners, and the WHO secretariat, to define the path forward for PRET, including the development of a global implementation roadmap for respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. The PRET Initiative’s first module focuses on respiratory pathogens, including influenza, coronaviruses, and respiratory syncytial virus. Given the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic and the possible threat of avian influenza, this module will enable countries to critically review, test, and update their respiratory pandemic planning efforts to ensure they have the functional capacities and capabilities in place. A process is underway to identify the next group of pathogens, such as arboviruses, to be addressed under this initiative. This will follow priorities identified through the ten proposals to strengthen the global architecture for health emergency preparedness, response, and resilience (HEPR). The PRET Initiative ushers in a new era for pandemic preparedness and represents an evolution of WHO’s core activities to support all Member States in strengthening health emergency preparedness, prevention, and response capacities and capabilities. The initiative can also serve to operationalize the objectives and provisions of the Pandemic Accord, which is currently being negotiated by WHO Member States. Following inputs and discussions among meeting participants, WHO invites stakeholders to this global call to action to advance respiratory pathogen pandemic preparedness. Call to Action
translations.hypothesis: WHO will continue to enhance and lead efforts to combat emerging infectious diseases as needed. The launch of the initiative took place on the last day of the conference, with over 120 participants from member states, partner organizations, and the WHO, to define a path forward for PRET, including developing a global framework for pandemic preparedness for respiratory viruses. The initial focus of the PRET Initiative is on respiratory viruses, including influenza, coronaviruses, and syncytial viruses. Given the ongoing pandemic caused by COVID-19 and the potential for future outbreaks of influenza, this initiative will provide countries with the opportunity to assess, experiment with, and update their pandemic preparedness plans to ensure they are effective and adaptable. Work is also underway to identify and address other emerging infectious diseases, such as arboviruses, under this initiative. This will build upon the ten key findings to strengthen global health security, response, and resilience (GSRR). The launch of PRET marks a new era in pandemic preparedness and represents a significant step forward in the work of the WHO to support all member states in strengthening their preparedness, response, and resilience capabilities. The initiative will also work to implement the goals and objectives of the International Health Regulations (IHR), which member states of the WHO are currently discussing. Following the presentations and discussions at the conference, the WHO has issued a call to action for countries to implement pandemic preparedness plans.

translations.source: Hukumar lafiya ta duniya WHO, Membobin Kasashe, da abokan haɗin gwiwa suna aiki tare don ganowa da aiwatar da ayyuka masu fifiko don ƙarfafa shirye-shiryen ƙasa, yanki, da duniya don cututtukan cututtuka da kuma barazanar kamuwa da cuta. Mun fahimci rawar al'ummomi da dukkan sassa, da buƙatar daidaitawa da dorewar ci gaba don kawo ƙarshen firgici da sakaci wanda ya zama ruwan dare a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba da martani. Mun tuna da darussa daga bala'i da annoba da suka gabata, musamman wadanda cututtukan numfashi ke haifar da su. Wadannan cututtukan suna kuma za su ci gaba da haifar da babbar barazana ta duniya tare da yuwuwar haifar da mummunar cututtuka da mace-mace, mamaye tsarin kiwon lafiya, dagula tattalin arzikin duniya, da kuma ta'azzara rashin daidaito, wanda ke wanzuwa a cikin damar yin amfani da kayan aikin biyu don hana kamuwa da cuta kiwon lafiya ga dukan mutane. Wannan Kira ne zuwa Aiki don haɓaka shirye-shiryen annoba da barazanar da ke fitowa a duniya. Shiri mai inganci ya dogara ne akan ingantaccen tsari da aiki tare. Alƙawarin gamayya kan ayyuka masu zuwa za su kai ga ci gaban da aka samu nan da Disamba 2025 kamar yadda za a bayyana a cikin tsarin sa ido na (Shiri da Juriya don Barazana masu tasowa (PRET): Sabunta shirye-shiryen da ke tabbatar da ayyukan fifiko kuma waɗanda suka yi la'akari da koyo daga abubuwan da suka faru a baya. Gane haɗarin da ƙwayoyin cuta na numfashi ke haifarwa, tsarawa don da annobar cutar numfashi dangane da jigogin da aka gano a cikin Module PRET #1: Abu ne mai muhimmanci zama cikin shirin fuskantar annobar cututtuka masu alaƙa da numfashi.
translations.reference: WHO, Member States, and partners are working together to identify and implement priority actions to strengthen national, regional, and global preparedness for pandemics and emerging infectious disease threats. We recognize the role of communities and all sectors, and the need for harmonized and sustained momentum to end the cycle of panic and neglect that is all too common in pandemic preparedness and response. We recall lessons from previous epidemics and pandemics, especially those caused by respiratory pathogens. These pathogens have and will continue to pose a significant global threat with the potential to cause tremendous morbidity and mortality, overwhelm health systems, de-stabilize the global economy, and exacerbate inequities, which exist in the access to both the tools to prevent pandemics and health care for all people. This is a Call to Action to accelerate preparedness for pandemics and emerging threats globally. Effective preparedness relies on robust planning and coordinated action. A collective commitment on the following actions will see progress achieved by December 2025 as will be described in the (Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET) monitoring framework: Update preparedness plans that affirm priority actions and that have considered learnings from past events. Recognizing the risk posed by respiratory pathogens, planning for a respiratory pathogen pandemic based on the themes identified in the PRET Module #1: Planning for Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics is a priority
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO), Member States, and partners are working together to implement critical interventions to strengthen national, regional, and global programs for the prevention and control of infectious diseases. We recognize the importance of community engagement and the need for coordinated and sustainable efforts to end the global burden of infectious diseases. We draw lessons from past epidemics and pandemics, particularly those caused by respiratory viruses. These viruses continue to pose a significant threat to global health, with the potential to cause widespread morbidity and mortality, overwhelm healthcare systems, disrupt economies, and exacerbate existing inequalities. This is a call to action to enhance preparedness and response to infectious disease threats worldwide. Effective preparedness requires a robust framework and collaborative partnerships. The goal is to achieve significant progress by December 2025, as outlined in the Pandemic and Epidemic Preparedness (PRET) framework. This includes strengthening surveillance systems, improving laboratory capacity, and enhancing the ability to detect and respond to emerging threats. It is also essential to be prepared for outbreaks of respiratory viruses.

translations.source: Kara haɗin kai tsakanin masu ruwa da tsaki a cikin shirye-shiryen annoba ta hanyar kyakkyawan tsari da haɗin kai. wannan ya haɗa da samar da tsari na daidaito, da gudanar da ayyuka tare da musayar bayanai game da sahihan hanyoyi da ƙalulabe da damarmaki. Ware kuɗi na musamman, ɗaukar nauyi da shirya bibiyar tasirin annobar da kuma ɗaukar mataki ta la'akari da giɓin da aka samu a yayin wata annoba a baya. matakin da aka ɗauka kan Kwabid-19 'yar manuniya ce game irin nasarar da za a samu daga shugabanci da al'umma da tallafi. daidaito cikin wannan aiki na da muhimmanci domin shiryawa annobar. akwai buƙatar al'umma bakiɗaya ta ɗau matakin da zai tabbatar da nasarar ain da aka sa a gaba. ya kamata aiwatarwar ta ƙarfafi guiwar al'umma wajen gina tabbataccen tsari na yau da kullum da cin moriyar ƙarin dama ta ɗaura ɗamarar ko-ta-kwana. An yi wannan kira ne a lokacin zaburarwa da ɗaura ɗamarar tunƙarar duk wata barazana (PRET): Taron Tattaunawa kan Makomar Annobar Cutar Numfashi ta Duniya da aka yi a ranar 24 ga Afirilu a Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.reference: Increase connectivity among stakeholders in pandemic preparedness planning through systematic coordination and cooperation. This includes building equitable systems; conducting joint exercises; and sharing information on good practices, challenges, and opportunities. Dedicate sustained investments, financing and monitoring of pandemic preparedness with a particular focus on addressing the gaps identified during past pandemics and epidemics. The COVID-19 pandemic response has demonstrated what can be achieved with political commitment, community engagement and funding. At the heart of this work is to ensure equity to be ready for the next pandemic together. Whole-of-society action is needed to make the progress outlined in this Call to Action. Implementation should therefore strengthen the resilience of communities; maintain, sustain, and build on routine systems; and leverage broader capacities for emergency preparedness and response. This Call to Action was released at the Preparedness and Resilience for Emerging Threats (PRET): Global Meeting for Future Respiratory Pathogen Pandemics held on 24-26 April 2023 in Geneva, Switzerland.
translations.hypothesis: Strengthening collaboration between healthcare professionals through effective communication and coordination is crucial. This includes establishing clear communication protocols, conducting joint training sessions, and sharing information about best practices and emerging threats. Specific measures should also be taken to address the unique challenges posed by the pandemic, such as ensuring adequate staffing levels, providing mental health support to healthcare workers, and implementing infection control measures. The success of the pandemic response will depend on strong leadership and community engagement. A coordinated effort is needed to ensure that the measures put in place are effective and sustainable. This requires the active participation of the community in building a resilient public health system that can withstand future shocks. This call to action was made during the World Health Organization's Global Forum on Pandemic and Epidemic Preparedness, held on April 24 in Geneva, Switzerland.

translations.source: Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta yi kira da a ƙara yawan yadda ake amfani da abubuawan da za su taimaka wajen yaƙi da zazzaɓin cizon sauro a yayin Ranar Yaƙi da Zazzaɓin Cizon Sauro ta Duniya, Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) ta yi kira da a ƙara ƙoƙari wajen amfani da sabbi da matakan da ake da su da wajen ceto rayuka daga zazzaɓin cizon sauro. Yara kusan miliyan 1.5 da ke fuskantar barazanar kamuwa ko rasa rai sabili da cutar maleriya daga ƙasashen Ghana, Kenya da Malawi, sun samu kashin farko na allurar rigakafin cutar maleriya RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S). Godiya ga shirin na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya. kashin farko na rigakafin maleriya wanda aka ƙaddamar a 2019 na cigaba da samar da hanyoyin kariya al'umma da kuma ceton rayuka. idan aka faɗaɗa shirin, Hukumar Lafiya ta duniya na ganin za a iya kai wa ga ceto rayukan dubun nan yara a kowacce shekara. a cewar darakta janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), muna da kayan kai ɗauki da za mu iya rage tasirin cutar maleriya da suka haɗa da: abin magance ƙwayoyin yaɗa cutar da magugunan rigakafi da na gwaje-gwaje da na maganin cutar. waɗannan sun ƙunshi ingantattun rigakafin cutar maleriya da za su kai ga ceton dubun nan yara a kowacce shekara. akwai yuwuwar kawo ƙarshen cutar maleriya a ƙasashe da dama, idan aka ƙara himma wajen cigaba da kai ɗauki ga waɗanda suka fi fuskantar baranaza. An yi wa ranar cutar maleriya ta duniya ta 2023 take da lokacin kawo ƙarshen maleriya bakiɗaya: zuba jari, fikira da ɗaukar mataki. Taken, wata hanya ce da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ke son yin kira wajen amfani da kayan aiki da sahihan hanyoyi domin hana kamuwa da ba da magungunan magance maleriya musamman a tsakanin talakawa.
translations.reference: WHO urges increased implementation of recommended tools to combat malaria Marking World Malaria Day, the World Health Organization (WHO) is issuing a call for increased implementation of new and existing interventions to save lives from malaria. Nearly 1.5 million children at high risk of illness and death from malaria in Ghana, Kenya and Malawi have now received their first dose of the first malaria vaccine, RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S), thanks to an ongoing pilot programme coordinated by WHO. The malaria vaccine pilots, launched in 2019, are increasing equity in access to malaria prevention for the most vulnerable and are saving lives. If implemented broadly, WHO estimates that malaria vaccines could save the lives of tens of thousands of children each year. “We have the tools to drive down malaria, a package of interventions that includes vector control, preventive medicines, testing, and treatment,” said Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of WHO. “These are joined by a safe and effective malaria vaccine, which could save the lives of tens of thousands children every year. With sustained investment and scaled-up efforts to reach those most at risk, malaria elimination in many countries is in reach. "World Malaria Day 2023 is being marked under the theme “Time to deliver zero malaria: invest, innovate, implement”. Within this theme, WHO is urging more effective implementation of available tools and strategies to prevent, diagnose and treat malaria, particularly among marginalized populations.
translations.hypothesis: The World Health Organization (WHO) has called for increased efforts to combat malaria, particularly during World Malaria Day. The WHO estimates that over 1.5 million children are at risk of contracting or dying from malaria each year in countries such as Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi. These children have been the first to receive the RTS,S/AS01 (RTS,S) malaria vaccine, thanks to the WHO's efforts. The malaria vaccine, which was first rolled out in 2019, has shown promising results in protecting children from the disease. If the program is scaled up, the WHO believes that it could save hundreds of thousands of children's lives each year. According to the Director-General of the WHO, we have the tools and strategies to significantly reduce the impact of malaria, including vaccines, insecticides, and diagnostic tests. These tools can be used to provide widespread and effective malaria prevention and treatment. There is a real possibility of ending malaria in many countries if we continue to invest in these tools and strategies. World Malaria Day 2023 is a call to action to end malaria once and for all: invest, innovate, and act. One key area where the WHO is calling for increased investment is in the use of innovative technologies and approaches to prevent and treat malaria, particularly among vulnerable populations.

translations.source: a cewar wani rahoto na baya-bayan nan na duniya kan maleriya da aka wallafa a watan Disambar 2022, akwai sabbin alƙaluman kamuwa da maleriya da ya kai miliyan 247 a 2021. Reshen Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO) da ke Afirka ne ya fi samun yawan masu fama da cutar, inda aka samu alƙaluman kamuwa da cutar maleriya da kaso 95% (miliyan 234) da kaso 96% na waɗanda suka rasa rayukansu (593000) a 2021. Kaso 80% ne waɗanda suka rasa ransu a yankin Afrika saboda cutar maleriya yara ne da ke ƙasa da shekaru biyar a duniya. Sabbin hanyoyi da kayan aiki Wasu ƙasashe sun yi nasarar kai wa ga faɗaɗa kai ɗauki ga al'ummatan da suka fi fuskantar baranar kamuwa da maleriya. Duk da wannan nasara, da yawa daga cikin al'umma na cigaba da fuskantar barazar kamuwa da cutar maleriya saboda rashin samun ɗaukin kariya da gano da magance cutar. Kai tallafi da shafi cutar maleriya ya gamu da tazgaro musamman a yankin Saharar Afirka saboda annobar Kwabid-19 da matsalar jin-ƙai da ƙarancin samun kuɗin ɗaukar nauyi da rashin sa-ido da ƙarancin wadatattun kayan aikin yaƙi da cutar maleriya. A kwanakin baya ne Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta wallafa wasu sabbin dabaru da hanyoyi na magance barazana da kai ɗauki ga ƙasashe domin samar da ingantattun shirye-shirye kan cutar maleriya.: sabbin dabarun da za su ƙunshi magungunan rigakafin maleriya a Afirka; muhalli;
translations.reference: According to the latest World malaria report, published in December 2022, there were an estimated 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The WHO African Region continues to shoulder the heaviest burden of the disease – accounting for an estimated 95% of all malaria cases (234 million) and 96% of all deaths (593 000) in 2021. Nearly 80% of malaria deaths in the African Region were among children under the age of five. New strategies and tools Countries have made some progress in expanding access to malaria services for most-at-risk populations. Despite some progress, many people at high risk of malaria still lack access to services that can prevent, detect and treat the disease. Challenges in expanding access to malaria services have been compounded, particularly in sub-Saharan Africa, by the COVID-19 pandemic, converging humanitarian crises, restricted funding, weak surveillance systems, and declines in the effectiveness of core malaria-fighting tools. To address these threats and support countries in building more resilient malaria programmes, WHO recently published new strategies and frameworks, including: a new strategy to contain antimalarial drug resistance in Africa; a new initiative to stop the spread of Anopheles stephensi in urban environments;
translations.hypothesis: According to a recent global report on malaria published in December 2022, there were 247 million new cases of malaria in 2021. The World Health Organization (WHO) office in Africa reported that 95% (234 million) of malaria cases and 96% (593,000) of deaths occurred in Africa in 2021. Eighty percent of deaths from malaria globally are among children under five years of age. New strategies and interventions have been implemented in some countries to reduce the burden of malaria. Despite these efforts, many communities continue to experience high rates of malaria due to lack of access to prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. Malaria control efforts have been particularly challenging in the Sahel region of Africa due to the ongoing COVID-19 pandemic, food insecurity, and limited access to essential health services. In recent years, the WHO has issued new guidance and recommendations for countries to strengthen their malaria control programs. These new strategies include the use of new antimalarial drugs, vector control measures, and community engagement activities.

translations.source: wani sabon tsari na haɗin guiwa da aka samar tsakanin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya da Sashen Gina Birane na Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya, zai yi wa shugabanni jagoranci wajen kula da cutar maleriya a birane. a akwai sabbin kayan aikin da za su taimaka wa ƙasashe wajen auna matakin cutar maleriya da gano ɓangarorin da ya kamata a kashe wa kuɗi Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta ƙara faɗaɗa damar kai wa ga shawarwarinta kan cutar maleriya. ana iya zamun ƙunshin shawarwarin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya ta manhajoji guda biyu: MAGICapp da Malaria Toolkitapp. ƙasashe za su iya magance cututtuka domin ƙara cin moriyar shawarwarin Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya. sabbin hanyoyin kai ɗauki cigaba da tallafawa bunƙasawa da rarraba sabon rigakafin maleriya da sabunta fasahar kayayyaki hanya ce da za kai ga cimma muradan kawo ƙarshen maleriya na 2030. Nau'in rigakafin maleriya na biyu, R21/Matrix-M (R21), zai iya taimakawa wajen wadatuwarsa ga masaya tare da rage yawan yara masu fama da zazzaɓi da ransa rai saboda cutar ta maleriya. abu ne mai muhimmanci ga Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya cigaba da binciken kwakwaf kan nau'in rigakafin maleriya na R21 har sai ta samu rahoton ƙarin tabbaci game da ingancin bayanai kan gwajin R21 karo na uku da ake yi da zarar an kammala. ana sa ran kai rigakafin maleriya na RTSS zuwa wasu ƙasashen Afirka a farkon shekarar 2024.
translations.reference: a new framework, developed jointly by WHO and UN-Habitat, to guide city leaders in urban malaria control. a new toolkit to help countries assess their malaria surveillance systems and identify areas for investment WHO has also increased the transparency, flexibility and access to its malaria recommendations. The consolidated WHO Guidelines for malaria are now available through two digital platforms: MAGICapp and the “Malaria Toolkit” app. WHO encourages countries to tailor the recommendations to local disease settings for maximum impact. Prospects for new interventions Continued investment in the development and deployment of new malaria vaccines and next-generation tools will be key to achieving the 2030 global malaria targets. A second malaria vaccine, the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine, if approved, could help close the sizable gap between supply and demand and further reduce child illness and death from malaria. It is a priority for WHO to continue the thorough and efficient expert review of the R21 malaria vaccine once additional key safety and efficacy data from the ongoing R21 phase 3 trial are available and provided to WHO. Phased introductions of the RTS,S malaria vaccine in additional countries in Africa are expected to begin in early 2024.
translations.hypothesis: A new partnership between the World Health Organization and the Bill & Melinda Gates Foundation will provide leaders with the tools and resources they need to combat malaria in cities. The new initiative will help countries to better understand the burden of malaria and to target resources to where they are most needed. The partnership will also provide countries with access to the World Health Organization's malaria control programs, including the MAGICapp and Malaria Toolkitapp. These tools can help countries to track their progress and to identify areas where they need to improve. In addition, the partnership will support research and development of new malaria control tools. One of the new tools being developed is the R21/Matrix-M (R21) vaccine. This vaccine has the potential to be highly effective in preventing malaria in children. The World Health Organization is also working to develop a new tool called RTSS, which is expected to be available in some African countries in early 2024.

translations.source: akwai sabbin kayayyaki a fannin bincike a ɓangaren kula da kwayoyin da ke yaɗa cuta. Ga misali, kayayyakin da ake kan nazarinsu sun haɗa da, sabbin nau'o'in gidan sauro mai ɗauke da sinadarin kashe ƙwari, abubuwan yi wa sauro ƙofar-rago, abin kore sauro da gidan kashe sauro da sauya zubin halittar sauro. Masu bincike a ɓangaren samar da rigakafin yaƙi da cutar maleriya sun fi mai da hankali ga cigaban hanyoyin warkarwa da ba na ACT ba saboda ƙarancin tasirin sinadarin atimisinin. ana sa ran cewa cigaba wajen samar da magugunan da za su zama zakaran gwajin dafi a nan gaba kamar "Triaple ACTs" wanda ya ƙunshi sinadarin atimisinin da wasu magunguna biyu, zai rage fargabar rashin tasirin magani. abin lura ga edita: Domin samun ƙarin bayani game da Ranar Hukumasr Lafiya ta Duniya, ziyarci: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.reference: In the vector control space, there are 28 new products in the R&D pipeline. Tools under evaluation include, for example, new types of insecticide-treated nets, targeted baits that attract mosquitoes, spatial repellents, lethal house lures (eaves tubes) and genetic engineering of mosquitoes. Researchers are prioritizing the development of non-ACT treatments in the field of antimalarial medicines due to the emergence and spread of partial artemisinin resistance. The development of next-generation medicines, such as "triple ACTs," which use a combination of artemisinin and two partner drugs, is underway to reduce the risk of drug resistance. Notes to the editor:For more information on the WHO World Malaria Day campaign, visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023
translations.hypothesis: There are new innovations in the field of malaria research, particularly in the area of drug development. For example, new types of insecticide-treated bed nets, door and window screens, and indoor residual spraying have been developed. Researchers in the field of malaria prevention are also focusing on developing new drugs that are not dependent on artemisinin, as artemisinin resistance is a growing problem. It is hoped that continued development of new drugs, such as "Triaple ACTs," which contain artemisinin and two other drugs, will help to overcome the problem of artemisinin resistance. Note to editor: For more information about World Malaria Day, please visit: https://www.who.int/campaigns/world-malaria-day/2023

translations.source: Shirin Kamfanin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA ya shiga mataki na biyu a cigaban da yake samu. za a ƙaddamar da Kamfanin Fasaha na mRNA a Afrigen, a Afrika ta Kudu. abokan haɗin guiwar za su haɗu ne a garin Cape Town domin tattauna mataki na gaba na cigaba da musayar ilimi game da rigakafin mRNA. sama da mutane 200 masu ba da gudumuwa daga faɗin duniya da ke aiki da Kamfanin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA ne za su haɗu a wannan sati a Cape Town domin ganawa kai tsaye da juna. darakta-janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya (WHO), Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus da Ministan Lafiya, Dr. Joe Phaahla, da Ministan Kasuwanci da Masana'antu, Mr. Ebrahim Patel na Afirka ta Kudu tare da manyan jami'ai daga ƙasashen da ke ba da tallafi; duk sun haɗu a wurin taron domin sake duba irin cigaban da aka samu tun bayan da Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya da Kanfanin Haɗa Magungunan suka ƙaddamar da ita a 2021. Na yi farin cikin kasancewa a nan Cape Town tare da abokan haɗin guiwa na samar da shiri mai ɗorewa na Kamfanin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA domin tallafawa ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi wajen samun rigafi da sauran magunguna inganta lafiya kamar kowa; a cewar darakta-janar na Hukumar Lafiya ta Duniya, Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. Ina alfahari da duk waɗanda suka ba da gudunmuwa a wannan aiki wajen samun nasara cikin abin da bai wuce shekara biyu ba wanda hakan ke tabbatar da cewa idan aka yi aiki bakiɗaya, za mu samu nasara tare. taron ya haɗa da masu haɗin guiwa daga kamfanin kimiyya daga ƙasashe 15 da ke cikin shirin, da ƙwararru da masana'antu da wakilan al'umma da masu zuba hannun jari. Ana sa ran a yayin wannan taro na kwanaki biyar, mahalarta taron za su yi musayar hanyoyin samar da cigaba ɗorawar shirin kamar batu na haƙƙin mallaka da dokokin gudanarwa da fasahar mRNA da muhimman hanyoyin LMICs da suka shafi wasu cututtuka kamar cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki da kuma tarin fuka. Annobar Kwabid-19 ta yi matuƙar fallasa mummunan rashin daidaito na neman lafiya, musamman game da rigakafi.
translations.reference: mRNA Technology Transfer Programme moves to the next phase of its development Inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen, South Africa. Partners gather in Cape Town to discuss the next phase of the mRNA vaccine development and knowledge sharing. Over 200 international participants working with the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme met in Cape Town this week for their first face-to-face meeting. Joined by World Health Organization (WHO) Director General, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus and Dr. Joe Phaahla, Minister of Health and Minister of Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel of South Africa together with high-level officials from funding countries, this unique meeting reviewed the progress since WHO and Medicines Patent Pool (MPP) launched it in June 2021. “I am delighted to be here in Cape Town with our partners to support a sustainable model for mRNA technology transfer to give low- and middle-income countries equitable access to vaccines and other lifesaving health products,” said WHO Director-General Dr Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus. “I am immensely proud of the achievement of all those involved in this project; in less than two years we have shown that when we work collaboratively, we succeed collectively.” The meeting participants include biomanufacturing partners from 15 countries in the Programme, leading experts, industry, civil society representatives, and funders. During the five-day meeting, participants will share progress and discuss critical enablers for the sustainability of the Programme such as intellectual property issues and regulatory aspects, as well as the science of mRNA technologies and key applications relevant to LMICs in other disease areas such as HIV and tuberculosis. The COVID-19 pandemic has underscored that gross inequity exists in access to health products, especially vaccines.
translations.hypothesis: The mRNA vaccine program is entering its second phase of development. The mRNA vaccine company, Moderna, will be establishing a presence in Africa, specifically in South Africa. Stakeholders will gather in Cape Town to discuss the next steps in the program's development. Over 200 participants from around the world, including representatives from the mRNA vaccine company, the World Health Organization (WHO), and the South African government, will be attending the meeting. The Director-General of the WHO, Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, along with the Ministers of Health, Dr. Joe Phaahla, and Trade and Industry, Mr. Ebrahim Patel, of South Africa, will also be present. The meeting will focus on reviewing the progress made since the WHO and the mRNA vaccine company launched the program in 2021. "I am pleased to be in Cape Town with the stakeholders of the mRNA vaccine program, working to develop a sustainable solution to provide vaccines and other essential medicines to low- and middle-income countries. As Dr. Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus, Director-General of the WHO, stated, "I am proud of all those who have contributed to this success in just over two years, which demonstrates that when we work together, we can achieve our goals. The meeting will bring together stakeholders from 15 countries participating in the program, as well as experts, industry representatives, and community leaders. The five-day conference will focus on developing strategies for the continued success of the program, including issues related to intellectual property, regulatory frameworks, and the use of mRNA technology in LMICs. The COVID-19 pandemic has highlighted the urgent need for equitable access to vaccines.

translations.source: Tun bayan ayyana Kwabid-19 a matsayin lamarin da ya shafi lafiya da ke buƙata kulawar gaggawa a duniya a watan Maris ɗin 2023, kawo 69.7 cikin ɗari na al'umma ne a faɗin duniya suka kai ga samun aƙalla ƙunshi ɗaya na rigakafin Kwabid-19. Abin lura shi ne kaso 30% na wancan adadi sun fito ne daga ƙasashe masu ƙaramin ƙarfi (LICs). Manufar wannan shiri shi ne ba da gudunmawa ta baiɗaya wajen samun rigakafin nRNA ta hanyar ninƙa samar da rigakafin a tsakankanin LMICs da ƙarfafa haɗin guiwa a tsakanin yanki da sauran yankuna da bunƙasawa da ƙarfafa wa masu aiki na ƙasa ta hanyar ba su horo da tallafi. Abin burgewa game da Tsarin Musayar Fasaha shi ne tsarin sanya kowa cikin samun damar musayar fasaha ga mutane daban-daban wajen iya samar da rigakafin domin isa ga masu buƙata cikin sauri. A cewar dakta Phaahla, ministan Lafiya na Afirka ta Kudu; abin da muke gani a yau abu ne na tarihi, shiri ne da manufarsa karfafa wa LMICs da ke faɗin Duniya ta hanyar haɗin guiwa. Na yi matuƙar farin cikin ganin cigaban da aka samu a ƙankanin lokaci tare da yin maraba da tallafi da aka samu daga ƙasashe da dama kamar Afirka ta Kudu da ke da Kanfanin Kimiyyar Halittu da kuma ƙoƙarinta na ganin ta koya tare da musayar ilimi a kowa. a cewar ministan Ilimi Mai Zurfi da Kimiyya, Blade Nzimande, mafarkinmu game da Shirin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA shi ne ta zarce batun Kwabid-19 kaɗai. Muna ƙoƙarin samar da yanayi ne da zai ba mu dama a nan gaba mu iya fuskantar kowacce annoba ta hanyar samar da rigakafinta daga fasahar mRNA. Wannan hangen nesa ne da aka yi kan cututtukan da suka zama gama-gari ga al'ummarmu kamar maleriya da tarin fuka da cuta mai karya garkuwar jiki (HIV da AIDS). Haka kuma wannan taro ya fito da muhimmancin shirin da aka ƙaddamar na Fasahar mRNA da aka yi a Afrigen da ke Cape Town a idon Dakta Tedros da Dakta Matshidiso Moeti wanda darakta ne na Yankin Afirka a Majalisar Ɗinkin Duniya da Charles Gore, babban darkta na MPP da kuma ministoci waɗanda suka sa hannu wajen yanka ribon na bikin kaddamarwar.
translations.reference: As of March 2023, more than three years after WHO declared COVID-19 a Public Health Emergency of International Concern (PHEIC), 69.7% of the global population had received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. Notably, this proportion still remains below 30% in low-income countries (LICs) . This Programme aims to contribute to equitable access to mRNA vaccines by increasing the distribution of sustainable manufacturing capacity across LMICs, enhancing regional and inter-regional collaborations, and developing and empowering a local workforce through tailored and inclusive training and expert support. What is unique in the mRNA Technology Transfer model is the multilateral process that allows sharing of technologies to multiple recipients so that through local and regional production those in need can be reached rapidly. Dr Phaahla, Minister of Health South Africa, said: “What we see here today, is a moment in history, a Programme that is aimed at empowering LMICs through a global collaborative network. I am thrilled to see the progress made in such a relatively short time and welcome the support from so many different countries - countries like South Africa that have a strong vibrant biomanufacturing capacity and that are willing to work together, learn from and share with each other.” Minister Blade Nzimande of Higher Education and Science, said: “Ours is the vision of the mRNA Technology Transfer Hub beyond just COVID-19. The capabilities we are building are looking to empower us to deal with other future pandemics whose vaccines could use the same mRNA technology platform. It is a vision that has set its eyes on diseases that are prevalent in our environments such as Malaria, Tuberculosis and HIV and AIDS.” This occasion also marks an important milestone for the Programme with the inauguration of the mRNA technology Hub facility at Afrigen in Cape Town in the presence of Dr. Tedros and Dr. Matshidiso Moeti WHO Regional Director for Africa, Charles Gore Executive Director of MPP and the Honorable Ministers who took part in a ribbon cutting ceremony at Afrigen.
translations.hypothesis: Following the declaration of COVID-19 as a global health emergency in March 2023, approximately 69.7% of the world's population has received at least one dose of a COVID-19 vaccine. However, a significant disparity exists, with 30% of this number coming from low- and middle-income countries (LMICs). The goal of this initiative is to provide equitable access to mRNA vaccines by scaling up local vaccine production and strengthening regional and global collaboration. A key aspect of the mRNA vaccine strategy is the development of local manufacturing capacity, enabling LMICs to produce their own vaccines and meet the needs of their populations more quickly. According to Minister of Health of South Africa, Dr. Phaahla, "What we are seeing today is a historic moment, a turning point in our ability to respond to global health challenges." He expressed his satisfaction with the progress made in a short period of time, and praised the support received from various countries, including South Africa, the African Union, and the World Health Organization. Minister of Basic Education and Science, Blade Nzimande, echoed this sentiment, stating that the ultimate goal of the mRNA vaccine strategy is to go beyond COVID-19 and develop a platform technology that can be used to respond to future pandemics. "We are working to create an environment where we can quickly develop and deploy vaccines against a wide range of diseases, including malaria, tuberculosis, and HIV," he said. The conference also highlighted the importance of the mRNA vaccine program being developed in South Africa, in collaboration with the World Health Organization and the African Union.

translations.source: Babban Darkta na Afrigen, farfesa Petro Terblanche cewa ya yi, ɗaukacin ma'aikatan Afrigen sun yi farin ciki na cimma wannan gagarumin mataki na kammala tsarin shirin Fasahar mRNA. wannan wuri yana ɗauke da kayayyakin samar da rigakafin mRNA na Kwabid-19 samfurin AfriVac 2121 wanda ake matakin dab da kammala haɗa shi. Sama da watanni 18 Afrigen ke samun cigaba ta hanyar tallafi daga masu haɗin guiwa da mashawarta na wannan shiri. Ƙoƙarinmu na ƙaruwa domin kai wa ga ƙolokuwar mataki na inganci na rigakafin mRNA wanda ginshiƙi ga LMICs mai ɗorewa wajen samar da rigakafin mRNA. Afrigen ta samu nasarar samar da ɗakin gwajin rigakafin Kwabid-19 kuma a yanzu tare da samar da ingantattun ƙananan cibiyoyin samar da rigakafin da za a yi amfani da su a mataki na I da na II na gwaji domin kai wa ga ingancin GMP. Ban da wannan, Afrigen za ta cigaba da ba da horaswa da musayar fasaha ga 'yan abokan haɗin guiwarsu. Msu ba da tallafi sun taka muhimmiyar rawa wajen taimaka wa shirin da tallafin da ya kai Dala 117 zuwa yanzu kuma Faransa ce ta farko wajen fara ba da tallafin ga shirin musayar na mRNA. A wajen taron akwai wakilan Ƙungiyar Tarayyar Turai da suka haɗa da Belgium da Germany da Norway da Canada da Kungiyar Tarayyar Afirka da Afirka ta Kudu da Gidauniyar ELMA. Darakta-janar ta Al'amuran Yau da Kullum ta ƙasa Kanada a Kudanci da Gabashin Afirka, Caroline Delany ta ce; Kanada na ba da tabbacin cigaba da tallafawa shirin Musayar Fasaha ta mRNA, kuma a yau muna farin cikin sanar da ƙarin gudunmowa ta miliyan 15 na kuɗin ƙasar. Da haka jumular gudunmowar da Canada ta ba wa shirin ya kai Dalar Canada miliyan 45.
translations.reference: Professor Petro Terblanche, Executive Director of Afrigen said: “The entire Afrigen team are thrilled to reach this important milestone with the completion of the mRNA technology platform. This platform is housed within the end-to-end mRNA vaccine development and production facility where the mRNA Hub Covid 19 vaccine candidate AfriVac 2121 is currently in scale-up phase. Over the last 18 months, Afrigen has undergone an incredible transformation with the support of a network of partners and mentors enabled by this Programme. We have grown our capability and capacity to meet the highest quality standards of mRNA vaccine development, serving the objective to build sustainable capacity in LMICs to produce mRNA vaccines”. Afrigen has successfully established a COVID-19 vaccine manufacturing process at a laboratory scale and is currently scaling up that process to a level suitable for manufacturing vaccine batches to be used in Phase I/ II clinical trials to GMP standards. In a parallel process, Afrigen will continue to carry out training and technology transfer to the network partners. The funders play a crucial role in supporting the Programme with total funding to date at US$ 117 million and France being the first to fund the mRNA technology transfer work. Present at the meeting were representatives from the European Commission, Belgium, Germany, as well as Norway, Canada, African Union, South Africa, and the ELMA Foundation. Caroline Delany, GlobalAffairs Canada’s Director General for Southern and Eastern Africa said: “Canada reaffirms its continued support of the mRNA Technology Transfer Programme and we are delighted to announce a further contribution of 15 million Canadian dollars at the meeting today. This brings Canada’s total contribution to the Programme up to 45 million Canadian dollars.
